For #SciArtSeptember 14: Glowing

Many birds have UV-reflecting parts. In Red Grouse (Willow Ptarmigan), combs above the eye reflect much brighter in the UV spectrum in females and immatures than in adult males. Likely to do with carotenoids absorbing UV but read more at the reply: 🎨🐡🦉🧪

Point Lobos State Natural Reserve. If you haven’t had a chance to check it out, it’s worth the effort. All types of seabirds and other wildlife exist there. Pelicans seems too numerous to count, an occasional great egret are among the many residents. 
🪶 #birds #nature #california

My local lake is not usually a shorebird hotspot apart from nesting Killdeer, but the tiny beach area has been host to a handful of species this week. Started the day out working the sand for some nice low angles.
SO MANY photos to edit!

A Torrent Tyrannulet (Serpophaga cinerea) delivering some snacks to the kids. These tiny flycatchers (about 10 cm or 4 in) live on fast moving rivers and streams and catch insects right at the edge of the water. I found this nesting pair in March of this year. #birds 🪶#costarica

With the slightly warmer temperatures and finally some sun, a lot of the redpolls that were here during the blizzard, have gone looking for food elsewhere. There are still a few around though, and this male common redpoll arrived just before sunset to forage in the trees!🪶

This is Cassin's sparrow--a bird of the SW grasslands that, until yesterday, had never been recorded in the East Bay before. It was spotted at Albany Bulb in the am & several dozen happy birders went to see it. I LOVE how much communal joy a little brown bird (w/ a stunning voice) can generate. 🪶📷

Stormfly is not impressed with the snow, or with being asked to spend some time outside today. Though most migrate, some American kestrels actually stay in Minnesota all winter, which is a pretty crazy feat for such a tiny falcon!

This immature Great Blue is growing up! Its dark crest is coming in nicely, and those long stringy feathers on its chest -- used both for protection from rough reeds and branches, and for intimidation of other herons -- are starting to appear. Lost Lagoon, Stanley Park, August 27 🪶

A slightly nasal sounding beep beep beep can be heard quite often in our trees and is a sign that the little nuthatches are around! We get both white and red-breasted here and this little red-breasted nuthatch was brave enough to come and say hello!🪶

Black-crowned Night-heron is one of the few birds we've seen in Taiwan that *isn't* a lifer. But unlike home, they are amazingly abundant here. Herons, egrets, night-herons, bitterns, all super common and easy to admire. #birding

A lone Kentish Plover flying with Sanderling. 
Since 2007, the breeding population in Spain of the KP has dropped by 52% to an estimated 3,300 pairs. Heavy tourism, beach cleaning, extreme temperatures & dogs off leads are some causes. 🪶

It's #WorldShorebirdsDay so here's one. Shorebirds are really thinning out around Anchorage #Alaska (well still lots of yellowlegs around) so I may have already seen my last Least Sandpiper of the year. 🪶

🧪🐡A purple-banded sunbird (Cinnyris bifasciatus) photographed in Maasai Mara, Kenya. Sometimes called “Africa’s hummingbirds”, sunbirds fulfill the same niche or ecological role as their hummingbird counterparts. They are specialized nectarivores and important pollinators.

Great Blue Herons are typically solitary birds when out fishing. Very unusual to see two, let alone three or more, together, outside of a heron rookery. These three were part of a group of six herons fishing Alki Beach last summer: 🪶

Went to see my first Waxwings today in Kings Lynn, a flock of 16. What gorgeous birds they are, and so fun to watch them flying between two trees and picking the fallen berries up from the ground! Rainy conditions but still got a couple of alright shots!

Since you’re enjoying the red-winged blackbird pic (& name), here are some tricolored blackbirds. Diff species. Found mostly in CA. Grassland specialists. Endangered.

Similar to red-winged, but males have white wing patch (not orange/yellow) and females are darker with clearer eyebrow stripe.

One of the easiest, best things we've ever done for your yard is just deciding to let most of it be. We had a slim dead tree that fell towards the back last year, and this log has been a focal point for all kinds of wildlife, including this male Pileated Woodpecker this morning. 🪶

This palm warbler was one of a pair I saw zipping along near ground level, gleaning food. They eat primarily insects but will also eat seeds and berries. They also nest on the ground. Very un-warblery warbler! 🪶

Should probably do some bird-posting here. 

Superb fairy-wrens, cute little golfball sized critters that pair up for life, but then just constantly get amongst it: more than two-thirds of chicks are not related to the male of the pair

Three-cornered leek in the garden and snake's-head fritillary, Chiffchaff and Canada Geese at Lakeside Country Park today. Goldcrest, Firecrest, Reed Bunting, Redwing and Great Crested Grebe were other highlights at Lakeside.

I like this shot because it shows how well these special sparrows move through the marsh grasses. They're not fans of being out in the open.

Saltmarsh Sparrow
(Ammospiza caudacuta)

#sparrows #birds 🪶

Hoping to be encouraged by a special sighting yesterday on our weekly outing along the Niagara River, hubs and I were not disappointed. A pair of mature eagles were spotted perched closely together in a tree. We've seen this pair before but always perched in separate trees. Truly a gift to us! 🙏🪶

#BirdGlamour’s Halloween-colored birds continues with Black-vented Orioles! These blackbird relatives call Central & South America home but are occasionally spotted in AZ & TX. Black-vented Orioles love an open area with scattered trees. Their name comes from their black vent (aka bum) area. 🐡🧪

The mandarin duck (Aix galericulata) is considered one of the most beautiful birds in the world.
      It is a native of China and Japan.The first mandarins were imported to Britain in the mid-18th century, but it wasn't until the 1930s that escapes from wildfowl collections started breeding here.

Anna's Hummingbird 
           Relatively large-headed and chunky. Gray and green overall, lacking buffy or orangey tones.Males have shocking magenta head.
 Expanding its range northward into British Columbia, even regular in southeast Alaska. Winters to southern Baja and locally elsewhere in Mexico

Young raptors this red-tailed hawk grow up fast and furious! Fully grown and flying at just six weeks old, their parents support them a little longer, but they're often on their own at just a few months old. A few missed meals can land them in a world of trouble, like this starving kid. 🪶

Absolute scenes at Elsie Roemer today as a phalanx of 100s of elegant terns, Forster’s terns, Western gulls, ring-billed gulls, and double-crested cormorants swept through the bay. 🪶

(Great birding day; bad photo day. Every setting was off and I somehow turned the image stabilizer off too.😩)

I've developed a real softspot for this diving duck, the Pied-Billed Grebe! They're always so adorable and charismatic 🪶 📷🧪

these lil guys can adjust their buoyancy to float with their heads/necks at different levels of the water!

Dry bread makes your cheeks red, goes a German saying. However, cold air has the same effect. I look similar to this Redbellied Woodpecker after going outside to refill feeders and baths. Stay warm, everybody! 🪶

The Southern ground hornbill, the larger of the two ground hornbill species in southern Africa, features distinctive traits. Measuring 90-129 cm (2 ft 11 in - 4 ft 3 in) with a wingspan of 1.2-1.8 m (3 ft 11 in to 5 ft 11 in), it stands at approximately 100 cm tall.

Celebrated the holiday by forcing my long-suffering father to search for hours for Red-cockaded Woodpeckers 

We found active-looking marked nest trees and ~6 million Brown-headed Nuthatches but no woodpeckers

Learned afterwards the park has wild flytraps, should’ve looked

🪶 A few white-faced storm-petrels have been seen at Altona this past fortnight. This migratory seabird has a global range and doesn't make its way into the bay very often. After three trips, I finally got this pic with about ten minutes of light left in the day.

A great blue heron just landed on my head while we’re sailing. I… don’t understand. I wouldn’t have believed it if my friend hadn’t seen the whole thing. It jumped off since I was obviously very startled.

Sunday afternoon went birding with a friend in South Louisiana. As the sun was setting we noticed a large amount of Scissor-tailed Flycatchers. A careful 1x1 count and got us 53! As we were leaving they started to roost in the Oak trees. Quite a sight just an hour south of New Orleans!

Great Blue Heron walking in shallow water next to San Francisco Bay in a little cove right behind Trader Vic's in Emeryville. He was quite large and I was very surprised when I saw him right next to the boardwalk that is along the Bay (and lucky that I had a camera with me).
#Birds 🪶
#Photography 📷

We were sitting by the river when 5 or 6 bluebirds unexpectedly settled in the oak tree overhead.  I managed to get a silhouette shot of one.  Not a good shot mind you, but it was as good as I could get with my crappy phone camera. 🪶

I think Waterthrush Wednesday sounds like a great idea! Waterthrushes are some of my favorite spring warblers, even though they can be a bit elusive. Here's a Northern Waterthrush from last spring, showing off its insect hunting skills. 🪶🌿

„Small farms with natural landscape features such as shade trees, hedgerows and tracts of intact forest provide a refuge for some tropical bird populations, according to an 18-year study in Costa Rica.“

Sharp-shinned Hawks, AKA "Sharpies", are Accipiters. Unlike Buteo hawks, i.e. Red-tails, Accipters don't soar then dive on their prey. They hunt small birds so they are fast & agile in order to ambush & pursue their prey through trees and shrubs. 🪶🧪

A Montane Woodcreeper working on a tasty moth. The bird is bashing the moth on the tree to knock off the scales on the moth's wings. These birds hitch up trees like woodpeckers while foraging for insects. 🪶🌿🧪
Bellavista Cloud Forest Reserve, Ecuador

Almost twice as many gray whale calves were spotted migrating with their mothers to feeding grounds in the Arctic this spring compared to last year, suggesting the population may be stabilizing after years of decline

Say “Halo” to Harpy Eagel 🪶😁 from World’s Birds Park in Walsrode.
World’s wide there are only 10 of them in Zoo’s:not only because there are seldom-they are also dangerous for the humans. Its talons can grow longer than bear claws up to 5”long, comparable to a Kodiak bear’s claws, which grow 3-5 “

🧪🐡Played myself a bit back by trusting a forecast of sun despite being in the PNW, and ended up on a lengthy hike in a torrential downpour. In literally the last 10 ft of the hike I ran into this beauty, a Cooper's hawk (A. cooperii), in a branch a few feet above me, making every second worth it.

I was invited to speak at the Colombia Birdfair in Cali, Colombia last week. The birding was amazing, I was able to see and take pics of amazing birds, this is just the tip of the iceberg!
Cali is hosting the COP16 so if you wanna come visit this would be a perfect time!

Green Honeycreeper (male) 🪶

…so soft and gentle; they sing to no one save themselves. Not loud & boastful like the mocker; not full of chatter like the purple martin. The bluebird song is a kind & personal “I love you” that one must be close & quiet to hear. 
~Kenny Kleinpeter

💙📷 Western Bluebird male, aka Sialia mexicana 🪶🌿

Haotz up, Hoatzin? Well, since you asked, there’s a  #birdwatching🪶 (sic)  boat ride on Sunday out of Garibaldi, OR. 12-2:30, short and sweet. I guarantee if you don’t go you won’t see a Hoatzin—unless of course, you are in South America. You won’t see one if you do either, but maybe an albatross?

Swans in Europe, Asia, and the Americas are white. For centuries in Europe, there were idioms like "might as well look for a black swan" which was assumed to be impossible.

But all along, Australia was full of black swans 🪶 They're pretty easy to find here.
Minnippi Park, Brisbane
#ausbirds

Some bird courtship for Valentine's Day:
Western Grebes rushing
A White-tailed Kite prey exchange
Magellanic Penguins billing outside their burrow
A male Red-shouldered Hawk after feeding a duck to its mate
#birds

Polders Poelgeest NL light SW wind. Pink feet moving north this am another sign of spring + 1st black tailed godwit alighted on Veerpolder 3 white storks inc 2 bill clapping on pylon shelducks up to 15, 3 oystercatcher a snipe 14 lapwing south the female scaup again on Leede #birding#ornithology

Totally True Bird Facts no.82: most commonly known as the red red-breasted nuthatch, these curious, friendly, fearless, impossibly small birds were first identified as Happy Little Beep-Beeps due to the soft and pleasant vocalizations they make when landing at feeders next to human friends! 🪶

According to all known laws of aviation, there is no way that an octopus should be able to fly.

The Bumblebee Two-spot Octopus (Octopus hummelincki) is a tiny octopus species found in the shallow tropical waters of the Gulf of Mexico and the Caribbean.

A sea of shorebirds! I really hope I get to see this phenomenon again. Thousands gather along the Delaware Bay as they migrate north, eating horseshoe crab eggs to fuel their journey. Just a stupendous sight 

#birds 🦑 🌿

🤓 Also fortunate to catch the proud papa ladder-backed! Happy 4:20 out there! 

📷 Dryobates scalaris, ladder-backed woodpecker adult male, crown extended in response to a threatening Acorn woodpecker 🪶 🌿

Osprey build their stick nests high up and close to shallow water fish sources. It’s fun watching them hunt along a nearby river, as well as brood on their stick nest down the street atop a baseball field floodlight. #SciArt 🧪

A northern scorpion, Paruroctonus boreus, the northern-most scorpion species in the world. I found this one, who was quite chunky and so I assume was a pregnant female, on a rainy day in the Pryor Mountains of south central Montana. #Arachtober

iPhone 15

Last night, three Bufflehead ducks swimming along in San Francisco Bay at Emery Cove Marina. The little guy in the back was rushing to catch to catch up to his friends.
#Birds 🦉
#Photography 📷

The sun was out today, and the Red-bellied Woodpeckers very frequent visitors. There are several individuals around but this male always sticks out because of the “line” on his forehead. The female is a beauty as well with her blueish forehead. I love that these guys are hanging around. 🪶

American Kestrel

We found this pretty female murderbird along a roadside near Nanegalito, Ecuador. American Kestrels have a large range, from most of North American south into the western portion of South America and then throughout most of the southern portion of the continent.
🪶🌿🧪

What does a chickadee look for in a home? How about northern flicker, or a purple martin? The 1923 University of North Dakota bulletin "Houses for North Dakota Birds" instructed kids how to build bird houses customized to each species they might hope to attract. 🪶🗃️

Roses are red
Violets are blue
These Ruddy Ducks
Are in the lovin’ mood too

Ok, maybe it’s a little early for Ruddy Ducks to pair up, but pre-sunrise on rippled water does make for some mesmerizing and romantic colors 💜💙🩷🧡

My photos from earlier this month. Let me know what you think. 🪶

This birdo is Fawkes the Red-shouldered Hawk. They live in our neighborhood and is known for being loud and raucous. They once buzzed me on my patio and scared the crap out of me. Rude dude (non-binary use of dude) 10/10 favorite Hawk ever. #birds 🪶

Went for a walk in Tommy Thompson park here in Toronto. It wouldn’t have been so cold, but the wind was fierce. I think the wildlife (apart from birds and some muskrats) was hiding though the beavers have been busy.

Downy woodpecker & northern cardinal 🌿🪶

I got some photos of more distant…

During my recent visit to New Orleans, I had the pleasure of seeing Anhinga for the first time, one soaring above Bayou Sauvage Urban NWR and others at City Park, Audubon Park and Lafreniere Park. A beautiful bird.  #birding #birdwatching

The Velvet-purple Coronet is a fabulous hummingbird that looks velvety black in low light, but catch it at the right angle and their feathers shine purple! They are found in the cloud forests of Ecuador and Colombia.

One of my favorite local birding patches is a park with a mowed lawn, a few gigantic trees, and a shrub-lined creek

It's a great migrant trap, those shrubs get loaded with warblers

Today, though, there was also a gorgeous Barred Owl in the lawn trees, my first there! (photo taken from a distance)

A MacGillivray’s warbler unexpectedly hopping around in the Bronx. A vagrant species usually found west of the Rockies, it was found by a 13 year old on their local Christmas bird count! #birds #birdsnyc 🪶

Thought my walk at my local birding patch this morning was going to consist of chilling with some wrens and sparrows and then found a LEWIS'S FREAKING WOODPECKER, right here in town. Migration is wild, y'all. 🪶

🪶 When I arrived in Florida I made a beeline to the Orlando Wetlands for a short birding walk before sunset. I was greeted by this gregarious group of Black-bellied Whistling-Ducks enjoying a rainstorm. 
I love all their different expressions!

#birds Costa Rica has a bit of a reputation for attracting some of the worst types of western tourists, but if you get away from the resort areas the wildlife is amazing. Toucans on the osa peninsula.

This gorgeous little White-throated Sparrow, along with three juveniles, were foraging by the side of the trail. I hadn't taken a good photo of an adult since April 2020, when I was just a tiny baby birder! 🪶

-- Lost Lagoon, Stanley Park, Vancouver. October 6

He's probably not pondering trying to catch that big ol' crane fly(?) in the upper right of the photo, but hummingbirds do eat insects. If you love birds, gotta help conserve bugs. 🌿🧪🪶
Purple-throated Woodstar 
Tatama National Park, Colombia

Over the weekend @@87388 and I saw for the first time the elusive Least Bittern! She saw it first, landing in some nearby reeds at one of our local lakes, and then we both got to watch it fly from there to a distant little island at the center of the lake. 🪶

Bird of the day.. Waxwing (Bombycilla garrulus) We’ve had quite the irruption of these bandit masked bounty hunters this year! Our locale is blessed with juniper, rowan & mistletoe berries. They now move on to orchards new.. a joy all told these past few weeks..🪶

Great (very) Blue Heron in the early morning sun.

This isn't true color. I was wearing thick gloves due to the cold and apparently fumbled the camera's white balance setting a couple of clicks into the blue without realizing it. 

That said, I like the effect.

When it's 15° F / -9.4° C out, an uneasy détente develops around the suet. The Downy Woodpecker, despite being the smallest woodpecker on the continent, is still an insane jackhammer dinosaur, and generally everyone keeps their distance. This Bluebird decided to roll the dice. Tense. 🪶 #bird

I've seen 72 species in Texas. 68 of them were before breakfast today at Audubon's Mitchell Lake preserve. What a place! Black-bellied whistling duck, Couch's kingbird, Bewick's wren, loggerhead shrike, ladderback woodpecker, white-faced ibis & vesper sparrow were all lifers.🪶

Remember when we were talking about the difference between the red spots & beak lengths on downy and hairy woodpeckers? 🪶
1 - downy (single wide red patch) 
2- hairy (two red spots) 
3- downy (cute tiny beak) 
4- hairy (tree-smashing jackhammer beak)

I found some more photos from several years ago of the Great Blue Heron after he's been bathing and before his feathers had dried. Love his spiky mullet! In lagoon next to San Francisco Bay.
#Birds 🦉
#Photography 📷

Surfbird, a shorebird finely adapted to foraging on rocky coastlines in the surf zone, photographed today on the edge of San Francisco Bay.  Love the tiny water droplets beaded up on its back.

#birds

Happy double lifer day to me! 🪶🪶

Mandarin Duck and Greater White-fronted Goose in front of a park trash can because, well, it’s a goose.

Urban birding in LA did not disappoint. Thankful for the tips of some fellow birders and the delightful weather of a SoCal November.

The fish hatchery in Blue Lake, California is covered by a mesh of ropes to prevent Ospreys from filching all the trout. Purple Martins return every spring to nest in the pipes that hold up these ropes.
#birds

Some Dundee birds from today's sunny but cold birding around the city.

#Birds #UKbirding #UKwildlife

Shoveler pair back at Swannie Ponds, Kestrel in Eastern Cemetery and Stonechat near Stannergate again.

BIRD REPORT I was just now sitting in my office working and minding my own business when I heard the utterly distinct sound of a belted kingfisher outside my open window. No lakes anywhere near here. 

Migration is amazing. 

This has been your Monday morning BIRD REPORT

Burrowing owls (Athene cunicularia) are found throughout the Americas wherever vegetation is low, and soil permits burrows.

They're prevalent in the mountain states of Colorado, Texas and New Mexico, especially in area where prairie dogs create convenient burrows.

(pic: Wendy Miller)

Break is an enthusiastic go getter, to the point where it drives other Maximals crazy. Waddlepop is a bit more mellow, loving a good high five and chilling in the freezer. While Break tends to ruffle Wabblepop's feathers, these two cool birds really are good pals.

Nice mixed flock of mostly White-eyed Parakeets (Psittacara leucophthalmus) with a couple of Red-masked Parakeets (Psittacara erythrogenys) feeding on the fruit of a Bullet Tree (Terminalia buceras), just outside my window 

#MiamiWildlife 🪶

Sunday's hike in Turkey Creek Sanctuary

Environmentally Endangered Land, the East Florida section of the Great Florida Birding Trail, the Sanctuary is 130 acres of wonder and natural resources. 

Established 1981, a boardwalk through wildlife vegetation and river views.

Mama cockatiel and her babies. Be forewarned....when getting birds, even though they're siblings, make sure they're the same gender. Otherwise you're likely to have grandkids. Birds are pervs.  #petpics #birds

#BirdGlamour celebrates the Spot-breasted Oriole! Originally from Mexico and South America, the Spot-breasted Oriole was introduced to Miami in the 1948 as escapees from the pet trade and are now are found in suburban habitats. Both males and females are brightly colored and sing. 🧪🐡🐦

Tundra swans! The ones I see here in Saskatchewan are on their way from their arctic nesting grounds to the eastern Great Lakes and all along the east coast, with most spending their winter on the mid-Atlantic coast. Fly safe! 🪶

Very cute little Black phoebe on a favorite perch under the olive tree in our backyard. Favorite perch, that is, unless it’s snack time. Then phoebe is usually in the back fence peeping 🥰 This one has been hanging around for about a month so far. 
Backyard #birds SoCal

A bar-tailed godwit developing its breeding plumage ahead of its big flight soon.
We don't often see this colouring in Australia. They fly way up north of the Arctic Circle to breed each year.
These birds hold the world record for non-stop flight.
Safe travels, buddy! 🤎
🪶 #birds

Another forest bird I was extremely fortunate to see on my Kaua'i trip just over a year ago was this Puaiohi, a very rare thrush endemic to the highest elevations of the island. In addition to its harsh raspy calls, Puaiohi sing a song that essentially consists of their name 🪶

Bird Set-up Photography (courtesy of hidden grape jelly [not jam!]). Hoping to see these beauties return in a few weeks! Grape jelly obviously does not attract Red-Shouldered Hawks or Hummers. The hawk was loudly screeching, I think, for a rat, or trying to attract a mate.

Cute Bird Alert! Get to know the Common Yellowthroat, a small songbird who rustles the bushes and gobbles up bugs. Here's two minutes with this cutie including a summery soundtrack of bird sounds. 

#birds 🌿 🧪

Red-capped Cardinals can be found broadly throughout the Amazon river basin. They belong to a genus of tanagers, not cardinals, because apparently we like common names for birds to be confusing. 🪶 🧪
Cuyabeno Reserve, Ecuador 2022

Finally! We've been in Virginia for months and have seen male cardinals only a couple of times, and only fleetingly.  We put a feeder outside my office window and this gentleman alit and ate a safflower seed. I need to clean this window!

🧪🪺There are over 300 different species of hummingbirds! Check out these four stunners:
1. Allen's hummingbird. Santa Barbara, CA. 
2. Anna's hummingbird. Seattle, WA. 
3. Green-crowned brilliant. Monteverde, Costa Rica.
4. Rufous hummingbird. Gig Harbor, WA.

#BirdGlamour celebrates Audubon’s Oriole! This oriole calls Mexico and southern Texas home.  Both males & females have yellow bodies & black heads. They used to be called the Black-headed Oriole: 4 male orioles have black heads. What name would you use for Audubon’s Oriole for the AOS name change?🧪🐡

I learned on Saturday that California has its own race of Red-winged Blackbird that has no yellow in its shoulder patch - the California Bicolored Blackbird.

I gotta admit: these birds were stunners. It’s amazing what a little color difference can do.

Woodland caribou on the barren lands. These two bulls were spotted in the Churchill Wildlife Management Area where they spend the summer taking advantage of the open area and wind to avoid biting insects.

This female Black-Throated Blue warbler snacks on a yew berry in preparation for her migratory flight south from NYC. She may spend the winter in the Caribbean or Central America, a journey of over 2,000 miles. She'll need all the fuel she can get!

Costa Rica's largest heron, the bare-throated tiger heron.

A huge wading bird of the tropics. The fluffy feathers already indicate how it can elongate the neck when it is in hunting mode 🐟

🪶
#CostaRica

Just for fun, I wrote a piece for Mental Floss on 10 facts you should know about Ivory-billed Woodpeckers! The most surprising, to me, is that there was a Cuban population that definitely persisted until the late 1980s (but, uh, it's probably extinct now too). 🪶🧪🌎

Hadn't gotten around to posting these yet, but we saw some good birds at San Elijo Lagoon last week: flying White-faced Ibises, a Belding's Savannah Sparrow, one of many Least Sandpipers, and a very pretty male House Finch 🪶

Here’s a young swan in London’s St. James Park. It was not fully white yet because it’s one of this past season’s cygnets. It was very calm and quiet, whereas most of the other nearby waterfowl were very animated and loud. I guess you could say that there was a low cygnet to noise ratio.

Phew, that's quite enough of that. You know who doesn't give a damn about "personal brands"? This immature red-tailed hawk. It was just trying to figure out how to catch a rabbit(?) in a stand of phragmites by the river, flying from tree to tree, and scaring the same four robins every time. 🪶

The Heist 🪶

Monk parakeets in an urban park in Buenos Aires were raiding the condiments at outdoor tables at a barbecue joint. They were always going for the mustard, bringing it up to where most of the flock was hanging out in a large tree, and making a huge mess
(rs from twitter days)

we get lots of raptors around our house, and although i generally don't care too much about bald eagles, i appreciate that they're a bird i can't mistake for anything else. there's no Davidson's Pale-Headed Megafalcon out there to trip me up when i'm trying to teach the kids what's flying overhead

From this month last year: just a couple of birbs being happy little guys.  These two immature Harris' sparrows decided to winter in my front yard; it was a real treat watching their adult markings gradually come in.  I wonder how they're doing. 🪶

Ready for release! This little hummingbird patient has been with us for a few weeks, he came in after a suspected window strike, thin and unable to fly. From Minnesota, he will join others now in a long migration south, all the way to Mexico or Central America! 🪶

“Only one thing has to change for us to know happiness in our lives: where we focus our attention.”
~Greg Anderson

👁️📸 Accipiter cooperii, Cooper's Hawk female, aka "Mrs. Cooper" 🪶🌿

Some yellow collared lovebirds in a secret location in Downtown Phoenix. 

These feral birds have been breeding there for about 15 years, but because they haven’t dispersed, they don’t meet the local criteria to be listed as an established exotic.

Been a long time coming this year, but I saw this lovely waxwing along with six others in Stevenage this afternoon, eating rowan berries and then attempting to control seven bad hair days in a nearby oak.

#Birding with mom today at Bonneville Fish Hatchery. We didn't see too many species, but the young 1st-molt Brewer's Blackbirds were out in great numbers. They are getting their first adult plumage through the grayer fledgling coat.

My favourite bird. Unless you are a bit of a twitcher, you have probably bypassed this drab little thing. 

But they are spikey as hell, and have... 'interesting' breeding habits. 

And a lovely song.

NEWS 

Federal prosecutors have charged two men with killing 3,600 birds, including bald and golden eagles, in a scheme where they trafficked body parts of the birds for a premium. 

Most of the bird killing was done on the Flathead Indian Reservation according to prosecutors.

Tried out a new birding spot today, and it was full of red winged blackbirds doing their territory calls and it just made me happy homesick (they're not close enough that I hear them in the neighborhood even tho this is like 5 miles away)

And look who I found sussing out holes in trees!

I hear it's Black-crowned night heron day. Count me in. They're such sleek birds. Found on every continent except Australia and Antarctica, they are the most widespread heron species in the world! #Birds

Woodpeckers can peck at 26 beats/sec. (faster than any rock drummer) and at >1,000x  the force of gravity. A human would be concussed if not instantly killed. I recorded this Rufous woodpecker one day last week, the call then the drumming.  🦉

Dad and Mom Mallard taking a relaxing swim while the little ones are safely resting on lily pads. Third in a series, that did not start out as a series. 🙂 My recollection is that either an Egret or Canada Goose was nearby and Dad was trying to scare them off. Lagoon, SF Bay.
#Birds 🪶
#Photography 📷

I saw more butterflies at St Marks today than I have in my whole life put together. Combined with the 6 alligators, 2 bald eagles, numerous ibises, dozens of egrets and herons, and a ton of other animals, today was an excellent day for viewing creatures.

So I actually went birding yesterday. I was looking for raptors mostly and saw lots... always from very far away. Oh well... my little family of Loggerhead Shrikes were doing their thing. It's very reassuring to go to their territory and find them there nearly every time. #birds

This was undoubtedly the most exciting nature experience I had this year. Two male Pileated Woodpeckers displaying at each other for over an hour. Circling, fluttering, spreading their wings to make themselves look big. And being completely silent the whole time. What a sight!

#birds 🌿

Birdsky, help me out. Is this a Barrow's goldeneye, as opposed to a common goldeneye? 🪶

- Same bird in both pics
- Arrowhead Marsh, today. 
- Merlin/iNaturalist both say Barrow's. Flatter head, lighter bill?? 
- I also saw some common GEs; both would be lifers

Good morning and welcome to your eagle of the day! 📸🪶🦅

These are two juvenile bald eagles fighting over prime position atop a dead tree. They are this year’s birds. You can tell by the black beaks. By next year, their beaks will start to turn yellow.

After a week of chasing Parakeets on my bike, I’ve finally located the East Oxford parakeet roost. The local flock of 35-40 birds all roost in conifers just off Church Cowley Road, opposite the cemetery. Now, is there a north Oxford roost too?

Avian field ethology.

Did you know that the Red-breasted Sapsucker, a kind of woodpecker native to the West Coast of the U.S. and Canada, only nests in dead trees? Its feeding behavior also can kill trees, since it involves drilling holes in trees for their sap, so they're real sickos.

Leveling up my birding while in San Diego for a workshop… paved trail skate from Coronado Beach -> ferry to Embarcadero -> skate through downtown back to hotel! A few of my favs = surf scoter (~75!), bufflehead, juvie Heermann’s gull, black phoebe & lesser goldfinch 🪶🧪🌎

It's blurry due to being taken though a window screen, but I'm BESIDE MYSELF at having captured this juvenile white-throated sparrow sticking its lil foot in the bird bath to test the water. 😂 The water was apparently not to the lil dude's liking as it decided to drink but not bathe. 🪶

Phoebe! Looks like it’s time for a few sips of cool water, then leaning in from the rim to splash a bit, much like the hummingbirds do - not jumping in to the ‘deep’ (maybe 1/2 inch) bowl. 
Then up to a favorite perch about 3 feet away to preen and rest. 

Black Phoebe 🪶 SoCal Flycatcher #birds

Saw a lot of golden-crowned kinglets in Redwood Regional this morning. Gorgeous, frenetic birds. The bit where the Stream and Tres Sendas trails meet is fast becoming one of my fav birding spots in the east bay.

Got really close to one of my favourite North American animals, the American Alligator. I took these pictures on a cooler day (around 16-17° C). I've found Alligators tend to be very passive below 24-25° C. I'm not sure I'd get so close during temps above that! 🌿 📸 🐡

My beloved Barrow's Goldeneyes will be heading back up to Alaska soon, so it was very good to get in the water with this them afternoon. The waves were up today, and I was working on capturing a sense of the ever-shifting, endlessly-dynamic environment that these wonderful birds inhabit. 🪶

Cordilleran flycatchers have been seemingly more frequent in the Denver metro this summer than usual, typically sticking more to the higher elevations. As years pass, older neighborhoods in town have more mature habitat for forest birds— it seems like some juncos and kinglets stick around to breed 🪶

🦉🪶 Beautiful in red, white and black. And quite noisy when they're nesting just overhead on your office roof. But I love the sound. Reminds me of the beach, even 120km inland. (scholekster, Haematopus ostralegus)

Just heard a Carolina Wren make a noise I’ve never heard a Carolina wren make before, kind of a soft trill, almost like a gray tree frog.  If I hadn’t been staring at the bird I would have been utterly baffled. 🪶

Mr. Blue Grosbeak seen at Sevilleta National Wildlife Refuge in New Mexico last summer. Rarely do birds perch so obligingly but this guy remained perched on the mesquite for about a dozen shots! 🪶#birds

My favourite corvids: blue jays! Brilliant mimics, long memories, highly intelligent, curious, friendly, and full of opinions - I love my little flock who come up to the window to ask for peanuts every day.

Downy woodpeckers are wonderful little birds that live year round in our area. Their sharp pip pip calls generally announce their arrival in our trees! This female arrived, to check the trees for snacks, just as the sun was setting!🪶

See the hole behind this hawk's tongue? It's the glottis (opening to airway/lungs) & why it's so important you do *not* drip water into a bird's mouth or force them to drink. If you find an injured bird, please contact a rehabilitator right away, its very easy to do harm when meaning to help. 🪶

Barnegat part 10…A😅: Long-tailed Ducks. One of two duck species this spot is particularly famous for. There were several huge rafts feeding well beyond the inlet of hundreds of ducks, but plenty up close, too. Took tons of pics, so first up are the males in their fancy winter colors. 🪶

In the setting sun, a pair of American robins puffed up in cold weather and sunning themselves as best they can in separate trees that are side by side. Wonder if this is the couple that built a nest last year on top of a dove nesting box and have returned for another brood. Hope so!🪶🌱

My hiking partner has been teaching me various species for over a year now so if I get one wrong sometimes she gets creative with clues. Yesterday she got into the tai chi position for a crane for the crane fly orchid. I love it when nature and peace come together with humor. Last year’s photos.

Brown creeper photo would make a devilish jigsaw puzzle! These tiny birds stick close to the trunks of large loose-barked trees, probing for insects. The start at the bottom and work their way up, then move to the base of the next one 🪶

White tern perched in an ironwood (Causuarina equisetifolia) tree, where the species "nests"... except they construct no nest to speak of! They precariously balance a bare egg within the ironwood's branches.

Photo from Saipan, CNMI 🪶

The Schleich 2022 caracal. Caracals aren't lynxes nor close to them, but part of the same lineage as African golden cats and servals. Famous for their ability to leap vertically and grab birds in flight, and for their long-tufted, communicative ears.

If you're fortunate enough to see large flocks of migratory snow geese, you quickly appreciate the different colour morphs: white (with black wing tips) and blue (dark body and white head). You might also see some young blues that are all dark grey 🪶

The leaves are coming down and the nights are getting cooler, but there are still a few of the migrating little ones hanging around. This dark-eyed junco is doing what it loves best - scratching around in the leaf litter looking for snacks!🪶

Feeling a bit overwhelmed with work and family stuff so made time for a cold dawn walk at Golden Acre Park. Not many wildfowl visible due to ice. Only some distant teal, flashing yellow and white. #birds

walk the afternoon out in busy local patch - midwinter vibrancy in treecreepers, 2 pairs of teal on the burn's swiftlead surface, a skein of pinkfeet angled NE - ice underfoot & clear sunset view through to Skiddaw's snowclad top #birds

🪶🦉I mean, why did they call all of our birds here the "common this" and the "common that"? This wonder of nature shouldn't be called a "common" crane. By the way, 50.000 of 'em are leaving Spain right now, to sail over France and hopefully Belgium on their way to Scandinavia. (kraanvogel, Grus grus)

What do Northern Saw-whet Owls & Barbie have in common? PINK! Northern Saw-whet Owls are very nocturnal, making it hard to age them by flashlight. The trick? UV light! Newly molted feathers have fresh porphyrins, which glow bright pink under UV light. Year 1 owls are very pink! #BirdGlamour 🐡🧪

Spotted a majestic grey heron chilling & taking off in high winds this afternoon (Water of Leith, Edinburgh). 

Phone snaps lacking decent resolution or shutter speed, alas.

Still visible: remarkable (to me) parabolic wingspan in last shot. 🪶

My only somewhat-successful long-exposure shot so far of one of the visiting long-eared owls in Central Park, hunting after flyout earlier this week. They are small and fast, and very hard to track at night! 🦉🌃

(5 second exposure, using only ambient light, taken from a respectful distance)

White peacocks can be found in various parts of the world, including India and the United States. In India, they are often found in places like Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh. In the United States, they can be seen in locations such as Florida and California.

Nesting pair: Today it has been raining all day and I caught one of them preening its wet feathers. It’s challenging to get good photos because they are a good distance away and they blend in so darn well, add the rain and lack of good light and it’s a bit difficult.
🪶 🌿 #birds #nature #wildlife

I do adore these little birds. Tufted Flycatcher (Mitrephanes phaeocercus) are small flycatchers that you find in the higher elevations of Costa Rica. They're fun to photograph because they'll use the same perch over and over. This was in the forest at Savegre Lodge in San Gerardo de Dota. 🪶

In great news today, both of the bald eagles were on the nest today. That's a good sign that they intend to hatch chicks in a month or two. 

They haven't been around as much lately, so I was happy. 

I need to stop and catch a picture of them, but it's an awkward place to  try to get one.

The Red-breasted sapsuckers are back. They aren't great for our trees, but I love them. This guy did not want his picture taken. It was raining and kept going to the opposite side of the tree to avoid me and my camera, but I finally got the little bugger. 🪶

Sneaky borb (sedge wren), knowing full damn well how tantalizing they were being (in the swamp grass nearly as tall as I am), making all the noises and rustling the grass and giving me precisely no good shots. That is how the sedge wren do. 🌿🐦

2023 favorites, cont'd: a juvenile brown pelican splashing about on the water surface at sunset. Likely trying to disturb some fish they can skim off the surface, which juveniles will sometimes do until they get a proper diving technique down. #birds

The cunning and mischievous cockatoos visited to eat berries, chew up the house and do squawking photobombs. Last pic shows one eating one of my grandson's crusts - not suitable bird food, even if they like it.
#birds

Last week I combined family visits to Scotland & the Lakes with my first holiday for a year.  Some great scenery and a few nice birds along the way.  Saw several Golden Eagles one day including the best views I've ever had in the UK #UKBirding  🪶

Size difference is a big cue in distinguishing hairy and downy woodpeckers, but they rarely pose side-by-side. Here's another tip! Check out the red on the back of the male's head. On a downy (L), it's one patch; on a hairy (R) it's two with black in between 🤓🪶

It makes me very happy that all the different types of the neighborhood birds mingle and dine happily together on my patio. This is probably normal, unremarkable bird behavior, but I will take any fellowship and harmony I can find these days.

The birds & squirrels merely tolerate each other.

This is a first...catching a raccoon & skunk at the same time as they're leaving the yard. Both are regular visitors with the raccoon enjoying its nightly feed of sunflower seeds from a bird feeder & the skunk digging holes here & there in the lawn in its search for grubs. #BackyardWildlife #Birds

Superb Owl Sunday.

Two years ago kid2 took a falconry class. This is Henson. He is a Eurasian Eagle Owl who lives at the Ohio School of Falconry. 

Henson is very chill and occasionally does acting work such as being in commercials and the occasional wedding.

While I was filming Mamita (the matriarch Baird's Tapir at Tapir Valley, Costa Rica) last night, my friend Donald was surreptitiously filming me.

The tapirs here are getting very relaxed with people, as poaching has been dramatically reduced and they're starting to see us as safe.

The tidal saltmarsh boardwalk at Huntington Beach State Park (South Carolina, USA) was damaged by a hurricane 

This Belted Kingfisher didn't mind having the whole thing to herself last week

(what great mascot potential, UIUC!)

A bit of direct comparison of two of the gnatcatcher species in coastal California

The blue-gray gnatcatcher, wider range throughout continental US, has extensive white on the outer tail feathers

CA gnatcatchers have limited range to coastal SoCal and Baja CA and show only narrow white edges

📷🪶

One of the coolest birding and nature experiences I ever had was touring southern and western Uganda a few years ago. It breaks my heart to see the virulent anti-gay efforts there undermine the opportunities for an exceptional ecotourism industry.

🫠 Today's birding included another Acorn woodpecker, a cute bridled titmouse, a moody raven, and the lovely blue squawking of scrub-jays asking for more peanuts. 🪶

You may, or may not, see pictures...🤘😶‍🌫️

Neighbor called me this morning as I was having breakfast.  Told me to look at the pond.  Rare sighting of a Bald Eagle just hanging out at the top of the big tree.  Not super clear because mega-zoom LOL

Near Yaquina Head Lighthouse on the Oregon Coast.

The little penguin-like, white-bellied cuties are the Common Murres. They lay their eggs on bare rock. Their eggs are unusually pear-shaped so they roll in a circle instead of rolling off a cliff. Brilliant! #birds

I looked out of the window earlier today and saw 2 weird scary brown long things on the lawn. Turned out it was sunbathing blackbirds. They were snoozing with their beaks open. Hilarious once I realised what they were. #birds

When nature made the blue-bird she wished to propitiate both the sky and the earth, so she gave him the color of the one on his back and the hue of the other on his breast.
~John Burroughs

It's early. Had to look up 'propitiate' John. 😵‍💫

Sandhill Crane migration is in full swing. Although they can often be found in open fields, huge numbers of them spend time resting in the wetlands and grasslands. You'd think that such large and loud birds would be conspicuous, but they're surprisingly good at silently hiding. 🪶

lololol i was trying to find a live cam of lake pepin mn to see if there were still any leaves on the sugar maples, & came across a raptor cam with 8 bald eagles scarfing some kinda kill, and a coyote dancing around behind them with a stick haa

Back in 1902, a much wider range of birds could be seen in cities. Horned Larks were a common sight on Pennsylvania Avenue in Washington, DC, along with Tree Sparrows and Purple Finches - all of which would be incredibly rare to see on Pennsylvania Avenue today.🪶

This great blue heron was wading in a pond that was barely puddle depth.  I thought to myself "there can't be any decent sized fish in such shallow water".  WRONG!  #Heron is smarter than me.  Huntley Meadows Park, Alexandria, Virginia, USA 5DEC23. #birds

Long-tailed Duck was our 150th bird for our Columbia County life list.  This one was semi-rare in an inland county. #birding 

Does anyone know for sure if this is a male or female? We called it male for the bill and eye coloring.

Kāruhiruhi fledglings on nest. Kāruhiru is the Māori name for the pied cormorant or shag. Photos taken at the Chelsea Heritage Park in Tāmaki-Makarau (Auckland). NZ has 12 of the 18 cormorant species, that exist globally. 
#addBirder
#birds

Yesterday while out with the chickens as they free ranged, we saw the world's tiniest hawk regarding us from a tree branch above—presumably a male sharp-shinned hawk. He was about the size of a pigeon.

Guys (Manning) will be like I know a place and it is literally just this dead tree over the bayou where there are often 1-3 green herons and they make the cutest squawking noise when they fly away (and they aren't even here today)

Adventurers Fen about half an hour before sunset. Hundreds of fieldfares and redwings in the scrubby bushes, struggling in the strong winds. A marsh harrier making it look easy. Great shimmering clouds of lapwings. And starlings gathering as the light went. A favourite place. #birds🪶#ukbirding

Borb No. 30: Great Gray Owl (Strix nebulosa)
Borbfact: Despite their large appearance, Great Gray Owls' size is due to the fluffy feathers needed to keep warm in their frigid habitat. Great gray owls are actually outweighed by nearly all "smaller" owls in the same size range!

#borb #bird #birding

Double the luck! First time at the sunflower feeder seeing the female red-winged blackbird join the male that showed up in the yard a week ago. Earlier in the day, a cardinal mating pair were at the same feeder. Now looking forward to two broods!😊🪶

💙 GM BlueSky! 💙

Although pairs usually mate for life, western bluebird females often mate outside their pairbond so that 45% of females have at least some nestlings that are not sired by their social mate, the caregiving male.

Does that mean he's f'ing around too then?? 🐦🪶🌿

We had the opportunity to attend a falconry exhibition this morning and it was very cool! We saw a Harris’s Hawk and an Aplomado Falcon. 

We learned a lot about the history of falconry and remarkable relationship between falconer and bird. 🪶

Three stunning thrush species from Backwoods camp, Goa. Orange-headed Thrush (Geokichla citrina cyanota), Nilgiri or Indian Blackbirds (Turdus simillimus), Malabar Whistling-Thrush (Myophonus horsfieldii) #birds #goa #india

A herd of Willow Ptarmigan foraging in Chugach State Park #Alaska They were almost finished molting when I shot this but were way ahead of the snow fall so they were real conspicuous. Since then lots of snow has fallen. I may try to visit them again this week.

From July 2014 and Jan 2015, Long-eared Owls in lean mode and floofy mode. Like most birds they will puff their feathers out to retain heat in the winter, and tuck them in during the summer, making for stark contrasts even though their body weight has barely changed. 🪶

#SuperbOwlSunday #owls

kirtland’s warblers are the rarest breeding warbler in the united states. they’re also tail-bobbing chonks with no fear of humans and a peculiar love of fire. this one has a pink mandible which means it was born just a few weeks ago!

Researchers surveying Ghana’s Mole National Park have found three critically endangered vulture species nesting there

This is the first observation of nesting hooded vultures in the park and the first white-backed and white-headed vulture nests seen anywhere in the country:

Listen, I'm not saying parrots are romantics, but when these two bonded birdies were let out of their (separate) cages this weekend, McCoy (red) spread his wing over Buddy (blue and gold) in a way that was protective and gallant all at once.

It's winter (weird duck season) in the Northern hemisphere, so here are two of the weird Australian ducks 🪶 from this area. Plumed whistling ducks - they whistle but don't quack - and pink-eared ducks, aka "zebra shovellers"
Brisbane, QLD
##ausbirds

A rare treat morning: mid-week birding at RSPB St Aidan‘s near Leeds. Soundtrack provided by BH gulls, Cetti’s warbler, and several booming bitterns (which remained elusive). Also saw skylarks singing, kestrels mating, 2 handsome black-necked grebes and my first chiffchaff of the year. #birds

So excited to get my @@421506 2024 Avian Ambassador decal today. Olive-sided flycatcher are beautiful, long-distance migrants, boreal forest nesters, aerial insectivores, and their call is "Quick Three Beers". What's not to love? 🪶

King tide Adventure! 

I went looking for an AMOY, but missed cause the king tides exposed more rock, it migrated to another bay north of where I was looking…

3rd plumage white-tailed eagle.

The white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla), known for its distinctive white tail in adulthood, is one of the largest birds of prey in Eurasia, thriving near large bodies of water.

Photo taken in Lohja, Finland, February 2024.

Scaly-breasted lorikeet 🪶 dangling by one foot from a tree hollow. This bird and its mate were inspecting this as a possible nesting site. The upside-down entrance was a drawback, but not necessarily a dealbreaker. #ausbirds
Brisbane QLD

This March keep an eye out for Great Crested Grebes throwing some shapes on the water! 

Their elaborate courtship dance includes weed waggling, rising out of the water, and nodding their heads in unison. 

We're BIG fans 😍

Good morning! This beautiful Grey Heron resides at my local park. He always sits in this tree while the sun rises to feel the warmth of the sun ☀️ You can see the sun warming his chest in this photo 🩶 #birds

I also heard weird noises coming from my chimney. It sounded like the crunching of bones.
I thought it was migratory birds at first, so two years ago I set up a sound-activated camera looking up the chimney to capture the source. It took this photo.

I do like this Mountain Bluebird pic of the one on the red branch. It turned to stare at me with a great #WhatAreYouLookingAt gaze and the wind picked up it's head feathers, giving it a definite look. LOL

🪶

princess parrots - one of Australia's least known birds, these nomads live in the driest deserts and every so often turn up in places where people live. 
This pic is from when i got lucky enough to see them in 2009 🪶

Four yellow-tailed black cockatoos in flight. 🪶

This brief flyover was the only time we saw this species.

From top to bottom, the first and third birds are females, and the second and fourth are males.

Spotted this handsome Limpkin at City Park in New Orleans. In Nashville, we have a lone Limpkin at Centennial Park that caused quite a stir, being uncommon to the area. He's still hanging out there despite the fact that the cold has set in. The snails must be delectable. #birding #birdwatching

The little treecreeper that visited us last week.  I didn’t know that the British treecreepers are warmer brown than Scandinavian birds, whose cooler colours blend into the lichen-covered bark in (presumably) less polluted countries.

White-crowned sparrow was an accommodating model, posing for pictures at the ODFW office on Sauvie's Island. Our target Harris's Sparrow (#127 Oregon '24) was more coy, and never gave a good photo op. #birding

Aside from mergansers, another migratory duck that replaces our mallards is the bufflehead. The males head feathers are remarkably iridescent, showing different hues depending on the angle. Taken at a distance, these photos aren’t as sharp as I’d like.📸📷🪶

Sunday's hike in Turkey Creek Sanctuary

Environmentally Endangered Land, the East Florida section of the Great Florida Birding Trail, the Sanctuary is 130 acres of wonder and natural resources. 

Established 1981, a boardwalk through wildlife vegetation and river views.🌱

I live on Lake Merritt and it is one of my favorite places I’ve ever been. The lake has a bird sanctuary, so you always see pelicans, coots, ducks, seagulls, and herons. It’s almost exactly a 5K around it, and I’ve been trying to walk it every day.

there's a pair of Carolina Wrens in the area that have been belting teakettle songs from the treetops and chattering from the shrubs outside my window. 

this is a different individual from a few winters ago, but is a perfect example of the tail-to-beak angles that make Wrens a favorite 🕊️

#ArtAdventCalendar 2023
Dec. 16
Rose-breasted grosbeaks are stunning birds that prefer woodlands and forest edges, so we don’t see them often in the city. This male was foraging deep in the branches but eventually moved to a small gap for a better look!🪶

#BirdGlamour celebrates the Altamira Oriole! This tropical oriole lives year-round in Mexico & Central America and they JUST makes it into southern TX/Rio Grande.  Females weave elaborate hanging nests up to 26 inches long. They love insects & forage dead grasshoppers off of the fronts of cars!🐡🧪🐦

Back with another raptor, this one spotted in Serengeti NP, Tanzania 🦅 I believe the ID on this one is a dark chanting goshawk (M. metabates). The chanting goshawk genus Melierax is named for their vocal behavior, particularly during breeding season 🧪 #SciArt

The Dallas Zoo announced this week that its first-ever whooping crane chick and three other cranes were successfully released into a wild population at the White Lake Wetlands Conservation Area in Louisiana.

So cute! Male & female cardinals at sunflower seed bird feeder. Male cardinal, who finished eating, flew to nearby tree to wait for female to finish. When too much time had elapsed (2 minutes at most), male flew to feeder with its back to the female in a "let's go!" kinda way. She got the hint! 🪶

High-density herons: a snowy egret, great blue heron, and adult & juvie yellow-crowned night herons all within a few hundred feet of each other at the Randalls Island Little Hell Gate salt marsh (10/8/2023) 🪶 #birds

Yesterday was International Tiny Little Bird Day, at least it was at Lackford Lakes. Marsh Tits and Wrens and Lotties the stars of the show (also Goldcrests, but you’ll have to imagine those) #birds 🪶 #ukbirding

The Black-capped Chickadees prefer to drink from a mote hanging above the hummingbird feeder rather than the fountain, and so are often perched in the magnolia's moss- and lichen-covered branches. #Birds in the backyard Portland, Oregon

I met about 25 new-to-me species of bird in Puerto Rico last week... here are a few of the niftiest ones I managed to capture! The nectar-sipping bananaquit (nearly ubiquitous, but hard to spot), the critically endangered Puerto Rican parrot, the scaly-naped pigeon, and the tiny Puerto Rican tody. 🪶

I'm not sure where this Bald Eagle was going, but he was intent on getting there quickly! He was flying at top speed over my head and I had just a fraction of a second before he went behind the trees. 🪶🌿

Herons in a human world.

Is “Spinal Saturday” a thing? I think it could be. Fledgling Cooper’s hawk enjoying what might just be it’s first successful solo predation (probably a house sparrow). San Diego CA. 🪶🦅📸🐡

A long-staying Ruddy Shelduck at Stoneybeck Lake, Bishop Middleham today. In County Durham, we get often a small influx of them in the late summer (most likely from the Continent) and in recent years one or two have been staying through the winter . #UKbirding #UKwildlife 🪶

It's about as far from the sea as you can get in the UK, and in the crowded south-east, but the diversity of birds around Oxford is just brilliant. Some of the highlights of the 167 species I recorded within 10km of home in 2023. No apologies for some distant record shots - it's birding!

The keel-billed toucan, also known as sulfur-breasted toucan, keel toucan, or rainbow-billed toucan, is a colorful Latin American member of the toucan family. 
                It is the national bird of Belize. The species is found in tropical jungles from southern Mexico to Ecuador.

This spring, when doing partial garden tidying, I left the dead swamp milkweed stems tall because birds liked to perch there. I'm so glad I did; I discovered that female Baltimore orioles carefully work the fibers out of them, creating beautiful long string for weaving their nests. Not only that, +

For #WorldShorebirdsDay I wanted to share some Sanderlings! These birds live on coastlines all around the world and are an icon of tidal shorelines. They also migrate incredible distances: some birds fly from the southern tip of South America up to northern Canada and back each year! #addBirder

I always enjoy seeing and photograph this bird🪶,the beautiful Red-ruffed fruitcrow, you can find him in Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Peru, south eastern Brazil and north eastern Argentina. I saw him the first time in my house and got enchanted with him!🤩 Have you heard of him? 
#birds #photography

Two types of lorikeet visit my backyard. Rainbow lorikeets are fairly easy to photograph but musk lorikeets are much more timid. I got a few snaps of this musky in my apple tree late yesterday.
🪶 #birds

Mourning doves first lining up at back of yard before flying to bird feeders. Their arrival at feeders invites other birds to come to other feeders in the yard b/c, in the bird world, there is safety in numbers. The flurry of activity at feeders & seed spread on the ground always warms my heart. 🪶🌱

Because I provide water/food/shelter to birds in my small garden, their babas learning to fly often end up in my garden - in need of rescue. This blackbird never forgot andhas taken to protect me from other birds, as a result - every time I sit in my garden ❤️

I'm dealing w/ blurred vision again since my last bout with Sepsis & I can't tell if things look okay after editing. So, if this is blurry, that's why. Anyways, here's an eye-level flyby of a Bald Eagle that I just got around to editing. Was standing on a bluff when the Eagle got real close. #Birds

A very lucky Rock Pipit escapes the formidable 2w female Pallid Harrier at Warham Greens, Norfolk. She always arrives at roost with a full, sometimes very full, crop. The most efficient hunter on the marsh. #birds

Quite stoked that I finally managed to get some decent captures of a bald eagle in flight today.  I've only been at this a few years and don't go out all that often.  Huntley Meadows Park, Alexandria, Virginia, USA 1-Feb-2024. #birds

A first look at a Sandhill Crane this year at the Lakefront. It will take a while to tell if these are the same mated pair from last year. Looks pretty good after touching down from traveling 100s of miles. Only stayed briefly with them letting them acclimate in their urban home.🪶

Probably a male Cooper’s hawk swooped into the yard and landed on our side yard. After staring at me for a bit, he was very curious about what might be in the Baccharis bush. In fact he hopped into the bush to explore. Came out with nothing. Hi, Gary 👋 Nice pantaloons! (2nd photo)

Thinking about how cute bowerbird courtship behavior is. Males build their little structures because the ladies like them, young males stop by when the adult isn't around to practice, and they like to collect pretty things. Amazing.

Amid the exceptional events of this past seabird season off the Isles of Scilly, it’s easy to forget that we enjoyed a record-breaking year for what is usually our ‘specialty’ species - Wilson’s storm-petrel - with an unimaginable total of 165, including 39 in one evening!! Another small brown bird with a chonky bill. Got my guide book out and I think it is a Variable Seedeater. They look so much like Darwin's Finches I checked to see if they're related and they are not. 🌿🪶

I love wrens. Love them. Nothing in the neighborhood is as fearless as this little rage nugget. Five foot Great Blue Herons fly away when you get within 20 yards; this guy will have babies in your bike helmet if you leave it outside and scream at everything that gets near. 🪶

Bird of the day is the Bohemian Waxwing. I only have crap photos that don’t do these beautiful birds justice. Rare winter visitors to the UK, it’s going to be a good year for spotting them. More than 1000 already in Scotland and northern England….but they’re moving south. (Alt for more) 🪶

I've been watching this young Red-tailed Hawk that has been hunting in the park next to our house. It often hangs out at the top of the sledding hill and has one distinctive red feather in its tail. 🪶

#BirdGlamour’s Halloween-colored birds continues with Orchard Orioles! Related to blackbirds & North America’s smallest orioles, Orchard Orioles don’t mind neighbors & will build their hanging pouch nests in groups. They feed on nectar & pollen when wintering in Central & South America. 🐡🧪

Eastern Bluebird, perched on a post at McMaster Forest, Hamilton, Ontario #HamOnt

Bluebirds love spots to perch like this so they can look down to hunt insects on the ground that they can swoop down and catch -- almost like being tiny hawks.

#birds #EasternBluebird

Sometimes I forget how lightweight birds are and then I see one just walking along on lily pads and they don’t even sink a little bit. This is (I believe) a female juvenile red-winged blackbird. This has been a random observation from your friendly neighbourhood swamp wizard

Bird photography tip 🪶 📷

With modern digital sensors, you don't want to be shooting in direct sun. The shadows are too harsh and the highlights too bright.

Try overcast (Townsend's solitaire), shadow (fledgling Anna's hummingbird), bright fog (Trumpeter swans), or rain (Little wattlebird).

A cedar waxwing in flight.  I've been looking for these birds for a couple of years and finally caught up with them.  Cloudy and shady, so colors aren't quite as vivid as I would have liked, but I'll take it.  Algonkian Regional Park, Sterling, Virginia, USA.  20NOV2023. #birds

During nesting season, the South Polar Skua swoops at penguin rookeries in an effort to dislodge an egg from a nest or find one unattended. The penguins make a joint effort to chase off the scavenger and protect their rookery.

Beautiful Brants.  These elegant and very vocal geese are one of my favorite winter visitors in San Diego.  These shots are from last spring before they left to go north to the tundra and coastal Arctic islands to breed. 🪶🌿

Cormorant comparison. 🪶 The big ones in front are pied cormorants, and the smaller ones are little pied cormorants. They're not often seen together as the little are more land based and the large ones are ocean-going.

A male Anna's is the only North American hummingbird species with a red crown. The jewel colors of the male not only attracts females but deters other males. He also sports a gorget, a patch of brightly colored feathers at his throat.

These Daily Hummingbird shots capture both! 🌿🪶

#birds of prey are definitely one of my favorites!! 🪶🦉😍 ....This beautiful migratory Broad-winged hawk showed up very near to my window a couple weeks ago. I live in Colombia, so you can imagine how special this moment was!!

We are working together today. I am also teaching him about European and British birds, but I don't think he cares that this is a European nuthatch and not one of the red-breasted nuthatches we saw occasionally here before we moved into the city.

i am a light vinaigrettes guy:

champagne vinegar, olive oil, meyer lemon juice, black pepper, thyme, and some good dijon mustard shaken together is one of my go-to dressings.

also don’t forget to salt your salad!

One of my favorite quick salads is greens (baby romaine here) chicken (vegan for me), and blue cheese stuffed olives packed in olive oil. Make a simple quick vinaigrette with the oil from the olives jar and lemon juice. It’s so simple and delicious.

A salad hot (correct) take: 

If you are making a salad with kale, cranberries, goat cheese, and candied walnuts, do not under any circumstances also make the dressing sweet. Goat cheese is not enough savory/tangy on its own to deal with all that. This salad is like eating the saddest candy bar :(

This dish isn’t terribly photogenic, which is a shame because it’s seriously delicious so I hope you’ll give it a chance. It’s a Greek dish called gigantes plaki. Dried lima beans are baked in a garlicky tomato sauce until they are velvety soft and divinely flavorful.

Kinshi tamago, also known as thinly shredded egg omelette, is a delightful Japanese culinary art. It involves cooking a thin omelette, then slicing it into delicate, narrow strips. These silky strands add a beautiful touch to various dishes and an interesting textural contrast.

Made the Short Rib Onion Soup from Smitten Kitchen yesterday. You spend 2.5 hours braising bone-in short ribs and—in the process—make the world’s best double beef stock, and while that’s happening you caramelize a shitton of onions and season them with sherry. Last: mix and top with Gruyère toasts!

Seafood Doria is a type of gratin that combines seafood, white sauce, cheese. It is made by layering rice, seafood, and sauce in a baking dish, and then baking it until the cheese is melted and bubbly. Seafood Doria is a popular dish in Japan, and is often served hot and enjoyed as a comfort food.

Two things people do wrong with French toast:

One, use thin bread. Yuck. Should be 3/4” thick, or 1”. 

Two, don’t soak it long enough. I’ll be giving my bread an hour to soak up all the egg. If you soak too short, the inside of the bread gets no egg or flavour. 🍜🍽️

My new favorite way to make potatoes. Get the small variety.

Boil them for 15 minutes (start preheating yoir air fryer after 10).

Drain them and let the water steam off, then toss them in some butter or oil and salt.

Air fry them on 400 for 10 minutes. Toss them halfway through.

Serve and enjoy.

the best caramelized onions you will ever make:

4-5 onions of various kinds, sliced 

5 tbsp salted butter

In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onions and mix. Turn heat to low. Cook for 4-5 hours, till onions are browned and sweet, with an almost jam-like texture.

Lazy porridge recipe!

- 125g salted butter
- 250g oat groats
- 1L water
- 0.75L milk
- 1 tsp salt

1. Fry the oats in butter, salt
2. Pour in water, cook in rice cooker on "brown rice" setting
3. Pour in milk, leave on "keep warm" setting
4. Eat when chewy & delicious (~6hrs)

If you are someone who likes umami flavor, can I recommend toasted soy sunflower seeds? 

Heat sunflower seeds in skillet until toasted. Turn heat off. Add dashes of soy sauce / tamari to hot pan. Shake rattle roll. 

Add to salads, pasta, wherever you want a salty crunchy kick. Keeps for a while.

Hot sauce taxonomy:

Yellow: Caribbean; mustard, vinegar, pepper

Chili-Garlic: Sriracha defines the type, but is its most mid member

Cholula-Tapatio: Classic hot sauce, more pepper than vinegar, often with some garlic

Tabasco et al: mostly vinegar

Beliezian: Like Cholula/Tapatio, but with carrot

Dinner at Sycamore in German Village tonight. Orange glazed lamb shank over goat cheese polenta & broccolini, curry mussels, sticky roasted carrots w Thai chilis & blood orange, & a mocktail ft Seedlip spice 94 🤤🍽️🍜

not to be a bitch but I'm having maple chilli soy mirin lime baked bacon on a salad of edamame, carrot, capsicum, coriander and spring onion, with soy-brown sugar tossed peanuts but you too can eat like this if you try to be a legend

Butcher’s mix (brisket, chuck, short rib) with caramelized onions and Gouda cheese grilled on hearty rye bread in Kerry Gold.

I am not a fan of "elevated" grilled cheese sandwiches. I dont fukkin need or want truffle oil or whatever. Just slap some nice cheese on some bread, slather it with butter and crisp it on the flat top. If you simply MUST do something to it, a little fresh cracked pepper is lovely.

I kept seeing these ads for oven ready lasagna noodles, especially one that featured a vodka sauce, so I was tempted to make my first lasagna ever. I don't think it's the prettiest, but fuck is it tasty as hell

Seems likely that this delicious, refreshing, very cilantro-forward hot sauce I bought in Boulder is not widely available outside Boulder but if it is and you like cilantro you should get some it's sooooooo good

The graham crackers and chocolate are the only defining features of a s'more. You can put anything in the middle, as long as you toast it over a fire. A banana. A tomato. A human thumb. Get creative with it.

Here is a nearly-ready bolognese. It simmered for another 10 minutes or so and then I let it cool and put it in the fridge. I’m going to assemble a lasagna tomorrow and then use whatever is left along with some linguini to make dinner. Notice the fat separating from the sauce. This is what you want.

Duck fat fries with garlic dip. (Dip’s a little fresh garlic, chipotle sauce, and 50/50 mayo/Greek yogurt.) 🍜🍽️

Made in the air fryer. Secret is: starchy potato cut thinner, & soaked in cold water, with a rinse. Dry them well before fat/cooking, & brush w more fat 3/4 through cooking them.

Not quite as able to cook a full meal without the aid of the helpful Sue as much as I wanted to treat her for all the help she's giving me at the moment. Easy clean-the-fridge Sunday night dinner: use up the sadder-looking veggies and make a scramble. Pro tip: make double and refrigerate the rest so your Monday morning breakfast is easy! Have it with toast or put it in a tortilla for a breakfast burrito tomorrow. #cooking

What weird meals did you used to cook yourself in your 20s?

I would sometimes buy a whole kilo pack of pork ribs, slow roast them, and just eat them with nothing else. Just a bowl filled with a kilo of roasted ribs. 

It was pretty good.

Have you ever tried #salmon #burgers?

This one I made was so delicious with my own special sauce!

For the sauce, I used sour cream, feta cheese, Za'Tar and Harrissa seasoning blends, lemon juice, and cracked pepper.

I toasted the buns, sauced it up, and used baby spinach and tomato as bottomings.

Now my dinner was great it was one of my packaged meals, but it was called jambalaya & while it had a sausage resembling andouille & shrimp & rice, there was no tomato base, there was no tomato, it was like pomodoro who? So I would call it rather INSPIRED by jambalaya than than jambalaya.

Made my favourite salad. Pearl couscous with roasted veggies in a balsamic vinaigrette, crumbled goat cheese and fresh basil. Great way to use up odd veggies out of fridge, here I had mushrooms, broccoli, red and yellow peppers. Tastes amazing!  🌱

Success! Chicken shawarma with homemade everything (flatbread, seasoning, sauces), the way the Lebanese parents of a coworker back in Europe used to serve it in their restaurant.

Starting off the new year with a healthy and delicious meal! 

I cooked vegan Black Eyed Peas with fried tempe bacon and chopped up shishito and poblano peppers with coconut, turmeric rice and Power Greens: kale, chard and spinach.

Packed with healthiness and deliciousness! #veganuary #veganfood 🍽️🍜

🍽️ Tried my hand at a traditional French Christmas dinner this year.  Duck Confit on puy lentil ragout, with homemade brioche rolls.  Also made potato galettes with smoked salmon as an appetizer, and helped my SIL with a Yule log to finish a lovely night. 🍽️

My weekday #pasta dish: Casarecce, tossed with succulent shrimp, garlic, chilli pepper, and garlic, then taken to another level with the rich, slightly nutty, smoky flavour of Iberico chorizo and a homemade kale pesto. Topped it off with grilled cherry tomatoes - bursting with flavour! 🍝🍤🍅

How to make Greek style roasted lemon chicken with potatoes!

1. Peel potatoes and mix with juice and zest of 4 lemons, olive oil, a spoonful of mustard, salt and pepper and half a cup of water. Lay on a bed of sliced onions and roast uncovered for 30 min at 200°C.

I rendered the fat from a pound of homemade duck sausage then sautéed (in this order) onions celery garlic parsley and thyme in it. Then added the sausage back in. This will get combined with homemade cornbread for stuffing

My own chili con carne recipe spiced with 4 types of chilli (fresh, aleppo, kashmiri and ancho), oregano, allspice, cinnamon, coffee, cumin, coriander and lime. Sounds random but it works! Served on shredded lettuce and onion dressed in a light vinaigrette cos I’m weird like that 😋😋🥰

I meant to post this earlier. Whipped this up to go along with leftovers. It’s a shredded Brussels sprout salad with chives, pepitas, and cranberries. Seasoned with fresh lemon juice, oil, salt, and pepper. I like this a little bit softer than fresh so I threw all of it in a pan and sautéed it.

Our lunch today was 🇮🇹 inspired summer salad: Steamed fish, boiled potatoes and green beans, red onion (added after the picture), local tomatoes deseeded in a dressing of evoo, lime juice, balsamic vinegar, honey and chilli flakes. Sprinkled with 3g of grated Edam cheese.

Burger King Chef's Choice Burgers (2011-2012): A "higher quality" line of offerings, featuring a 1/3 pound ground chuck burger, romaine, tomato, onion, American cheese, garlic mayo, and thick cut bacon on an "artisan" brioche roll. Variants were Bacon & Bleu and Bacon Cheddar BBQ

Scachatta! The Tampa Bay area is mostly known for Cuban sandwiches, but less so for schchatta. It’s similar to something from Sicily called sfincione, but schchatta has a bunch of egg yolk in the focaccia-like crust, as well as ground meat in the tomato sauce. Eaten at room temperature. Good stuff.

🍽️ 🍜 sloppy turkey, chicken, and bacon club sandwich with ranch, spicy mustard, muenster cheese, tomato, and dill pickles with an equally sloppy mocha i made from espresso beans roasted down the street

Make lahanosalata (Greek cabbage salad)! Finely slice cabbage, carrot and onion. Mix in 1/4 cup salt. Leave for 2 hours, kneading every 30 min. Rinse twice with water. Squeeze out as much liquid as poss. Store with a  few spoonfuls of lemon juice for max 5 days. Serve with a dribble of olive oil. 🥰

Shakshuka! Or spicy eggs the way I learned to make it from the Mama/housecleaner at the Moroccan guesthouse I stayed in for a month. As she served it, with an avocado mash, yogurt, and then my toasted homemade sourdough. 🍜

Indulging my theory that pineapple basil salad dressing would make an amazing chopped salad with a pizza vibe. Marinated chickpeas, crispy turkey pepperoni, ham, mini peppers, a dash of Parmesan and chili flake on chopped romaine, iceberg and radicchio. It turned out amazing. 🌱🍽️

Grocery had some gorgeous organic grape tomatoes. Cut up some basil and garlic with olive oil, placed sliced tomatoes over them and slow roasted for an hour. 

A little salt and pepper. Flavor is so good.

Carbonara. Fresh pasta. Using bacon, like a savage. Ain’t buying guanciale in this economy for a bit. Mix of pecorino and parmigiano.

Long time followers may remember an old post(yesterday) about breakfast mac&cheese. Here's a big batch of my harvest Mac. The sauce is half normal mornay, half blended butternut squash. It's super rich, creamy, & cheesy. Added some bacon and spinach to pretend I'm healthy. 🍜

Lovely lunch at Auberge du Boisne. Starter was all my fave things on a plate (taramasalata, trout roe and fennel), not pictured is my main of chou farci (delicious but hard to photograph) and they had a wine I liked 🥰🍽

successful replication of my favorite breakfast dad used to make. feta and izmir tulumu (a salty, dense, tangy sheep's milk cheese from my hometown) mixed with an egg, parsley, pepper, spread over thick slices of white bread broiled in the toaster oven. tastes exactly like my childhood 🥰

step two is assembling your spice melange

Nero di Seppia (squid ink spaghetti) with prawns tossed in a garlic, herb butter sauce, sprinkled with crushed pistachios with Parmigiana Reggiano toast on the side. Full bellies here & smiles all around😊. 🍽️🍜🍔🍕

Albacore Tuna Steaks cooked w/ assorted mushrooms, garlic & chives sautéed in Worcestershire sauce, Roasted Brussel Sprouts, and a mixed green salad with Cajun shrimp, Kalamata olives
