I love making a one sheet dinner… pork loin cultlets with Rosemary EVOO Herbs de Provence (Trader Joe’s) smoked paprika, tri-color potatoe babies, colored carrots… Bake! I’ve been under the weather for too long- it feels good to actually cook a full meal! 🙏♥️✨

I made my first challah bread tonight. It went rather well! I may have over-proofed it a bit, since it split more than I was expecting in the middle. Or maybe I should braid it less tight? Might experiment with turning the oven temp down a touch.

Tastes great, though!

Berber eggs! Made with some homegrown tomatoes so the colour is a bit crazy. 

There’s a spicy green roma tomato in there too. Spicy as hell with ginger, garlic, turmeric, cumin, chili powder, etc. 🌱🍽️🍜

Homemade on a Budget: The perfect loaf of rustic bread to go with dinner. Vegan, only 4 ingredients, and no yeast or bread machine required! Bye-bye expensive, processed store-bought bread! Living frugally has its benefits, and learning how to be resourceful is definitely one of them.

Two more cheeses from Piedmont Italy.
Top: Toma Piedmontese: a buttery cow's milk cheese with a rich salty mushroomy flavor. It's very savory and good.
Bottom: Breise. Kind of a fontina d'aosta clone. I think this one was a little too young and is a little bland to my taste. Comparable to gouda.

Flatbread for dinner! A no-knead dough piled with a mighty heap of halved cherry tomatoes, Kalamata olives, quartered artichoke hearts, sliced mushrooms, diced garlic, sliced red bell pepper, finely diced broccoli, and sliced fresh basil. Side of spicy marinara for dipping.

There are many egg dishes out there, but all things considered, my favorite might just be Dashimaki, which is a Japanese rolled omelet made with dashi. Having Dashimaki with grated daikon radish for breakfast is truly wonderful!

Probably making a tuna salad tomorrow that will feed me for the next few days. It's going to be lit & banging. My tuna salad has celery, old bay seasoning, black pepper, kalamata olives, carrots, mayo, & boiled eggs.

We ran out of hashbrowns, which is devastating to my potato-loving 7 year old. 

However, less than 20 minutes after peeling 2 potatoes she is eating the crispiest home fries with the fluffiest insides, thanks to the air fryer. Those things are magic.

So in ‘attempts to feel a bit more like my old self’ I did a roast. And I reckon it was the best roast I’ve ever done. Perfect pork, risen yorkies, fluffy yet crisp potatoes, roasted parsnips and carrots. All with homemade white wine gravy and apple sauce. And for 20 mins some of the gloom lifted.

First time making a French omelette, made Syd’s omelette from #TheBear, complete with broken potato chips. Not horrible looking for a first try, and it tastes amazing. (The one I made Mike folded better, but was browned when it’s not supposed to be.)

Trying to do some self care, so I made this pretty beef fajita bowl with some cilantro lime rice. Having a big glass of orange juice with it, to really hit my body with some good vitamins and minerals and stuff.

Today's supper (nothing fancy): a variant on traditional colcannon. In this case, mashed potatoes made with hot milk and butter, chopped bacon, and shredded Baby Gem cabbage fried in bacon fat: with a well in the middle of the colcannon, and more butter melting in it. (Because Ireland.)  :)

Breakfast this morning is whole wheat banana chai waffles with turkey bacon and scrambled eggs. The waffles are 1c wheat flour, 1 ripe banana, 2tsp baking powder, 1 egg, 3/4c milk, 1/2c unsweetened applesauce and chai spice. 😍🍽️🍜

Another messy lunch: spaghetti topped with various roasted squashes & crispy fennel (all from the community farm!), smoked tofu, fresh lemon juice and a sprinkling of fennel fronds, black pepper and vegan parmesan 🍜 🍽️ 🌱 

Simple, nutritious & planet-friendly 🌎✌️

Pic from my mom: she has once again made salenjaci, the specialty of Slavonia, the region of Croatia we’re from. They’re like croissant turnovers, made of dough laminated with leaf lard—unrendered, spread pork fat. They don’t taste porky, but they have a unique creaminess.

I do most of the cookin' aroun' heah, but my husband @@66593 is an excellent chef when he has a mind to do it. Tonight he made chicken Marsala with an incredible, long-simmered sauce with porcini, pancetta, and crimini mushrooms, along w/Brussels sprouts w/pecorino and lemon. Yum!

ERMAGERD CHEESEBERD 2023! Cheeses: Irish cheddar, Uplands (Gruyere-ish), Ricotta, Fromage d'affinois, standard cheese ball. Meats: Genoa salami and prosciutto.  Fruits: honeycrisp apples and red grapes. Extras: almonds, cornichons, pickled beets, tart cherry spread, black olive spread.

I take some pride in my home-made nacho game. 

I make queso with a roux, Monterey Jack, Velveeta, and chipotles in adobo, then layer it with meat, (leftover steak or chorizo is best), fresh and pickled jalapeños and a combo of salsa verde and nacho Doritos (or whatever)

Broil then add toppings.

little jazz in the kitchen tonight with some shrimp taco fusion off the dome, complete with orange pico de gallo and an orange-gochujang tartar sauce which went even better than expected. shout out to my neighbor who grew the jalapeños

Rumbledethumps a - traditional Scottish dish of potato, swede & savoy cabbage - cooked, mashed with butter, covered with grated cheese & then baked. Normally accompanies haggis, but not tonight. Supper tonight included spicy beans, pan fried haloumi & bacon. The rumbledethumps were fab 🍜🍽

ok back to loaded fries. home grown potatoes, peeled & sliced & boiled then drained & tossed in olive oil & smoked garlic salt. then grated (dairy free) cheese on top with chopped ham, spring onion from garden, chopped shallot from garden, with homemade guacamole and sour coconut yoghurt & hot sauce

Stir-fried squid with holy basil is a popular dish in Thailand, but it's especially delicious when eaten near the sea. The fresh seafood and the spicy flavors combine to create a truly unforgettable experience. #TasteTheDifference #AuthenticFood

lao ganma (“old godmother”) is a chinese brand of fried chili crisp condiments (deep fried chili flakes in oil). it is a potent flavor enhancer

white rice + spoonful of lao ganma + fried egg on top is a class chinese college dorm room meal

This time it’s κολοκυθόριζο (courgette rice) which is about as simple as it gets - onion, garlic, beef mince, tomato, courgette and medium grain rice. Served with a dollop of Greek yogurt.

Decided to invite family over for breakfast instead of deal with three toddlers at a restaurant on Sunday morning, so fresh corned beef hash, a strawberry and pineapple fruit salad, and of course, a big plate of buttermilk pancakes.

Mom made… well, I’m loath to call it paella as its not got Chicken/Chorizo, but it has paella rice, prawns, hake, wild salmon, garlic, peppers etc & a 1/4 bottle of white wine, stock, last night’s lamb fat. We soaked the saffron.  Calling it a seafood risotto wouldn’t irk the purists. #Homecooking

Mia en place for a simple Chicken Cacciatore. That little block in front of the chicken is my secret to making really deep, rich tomato sauces. A hunk of the rind from Parmigiano Reggiano helps offset the often flat flavor of canned tomatoes. 🍽️🍝

Important culinary secret for tomato-based sauces: Gochujang. Bolognese? Gochujang. Arrabiata? More gochujang. Chili of any description? You guessed it, gochujang. It hurts to type this because of my finger, but it's worth it to tell you this

Fried egg sandwich, egg was fried in steak fond and seasoned with white pepper and mexican oregano, on toasted sourdough with a sauce made from 1tsp mayonnaise, 1tsp crema, glug of olive oil with powdered garlic and italian seasoning. Blowin’ my tits off ova here

Sunny Seoul Hash from First Watch.  Shaved seared pork tossed with freshly seasoned potatoes, diced bell peppers, kimchi, Cheddar and Monterrey Jack.  Topped with two over-hard eggs, scallions, sesame seeds and gochunang aioli.

St. Patrick's Day. I finally got up the courage to cook my own corned beef. Vegetarian mixed bean chili, baguettes fresh out of the oven, sour cream, scallion, herbs, pickled cabbage and raw almonds. Turns out that was just the right amount of Guinness after all. Absolutely mouth watering 🤤 🍜 Happy autumn, folks!

The menu this week  mac + cheese Casserole, red beans and sausage with rice, nachos, meatball subs and a veggies soup.

Take a steaming bowl of hot rice. Crack a raw egg into it, add a little soy sauce, and mix it up. That's Tamago (Kake) Gohan, the breakfast of champions. To up your TKG game, separate the egg first, whip the whites, mix them in, then mix in the yolk. I once ate this for breakfast for 6m straight.

Daughter asked for a savory breakfast this morning, so I made biscuits (sadly with all-purpose flour, but my wife's visiting her mom and I'll ask her to bring back a some White Lily) and eggs.

Last night the spousy was craving chicken Caesar so we made some from scratch. PSA: it’s 👏 not 👏 difficult to make your own mayo and it literally tastes so much better. With homemade croutons, romaine, parm, and grilled chicken on a roll. Light and satisfying! #cooking #salad 🍽️🥖

Rushed this in 45 minutes, so it's not pretty, but it's good.

A simple salad with spring greens and sugar bomb tomatoes with a lemon olive oil vinaigrette, pasta tossed in olive oil and extra pesto at the end, and chicken coated with a homemade sundried tomato shallot cream sauce with white wine.

Some spring onions rejuvenating. Each time the intensity of flavour increases. Super easy to do with supermarket bought ones if you’ve not got any homegrown ones. Just rehydrate the roots for 24hrs & plant. 🌱

I have discovered how pleasurable it is to make a salad, esp if you are just a little bit high. No burner or oven rushing the process along. Just picking and chopping and washing things, then putting them in a bowl. This could take seven minutes or two hours and it doesn’t matter

Much as I’d love to be able to stretch to rib of beef, done bleeding-rare, the rolled brisket  just put in the oven will also be scrumptious slowly pot-roasted until it’s lovely and tender with Yorkshire puds and all the trimmings (and a lot more budget-friendly!).

Howdy, Bluesky. I cook a lot of meat and tend to make tacos with it! (From left: Smoked pork taco topped with caramelized red onion and pineapple, classic Texas brisket, ribeye tacos with salsa verde and just a plain old, juicy medium ribeye.)

Food & Wine promised “the secret to the best most classic version” of carbonara and whaddaya know, they also use my “temper pasta water into the eggs” method. I do use bacon most of the time because my regular stores don’t carry guanciale. 🍜🍽️

🌱🍜🍽️ Made a fairly rough and ready Tomatillo Salsa for dipping with my own homegrown Pineapple Tomatillos. It tastes pretty good with plain Tortilla chips, a nice heat but probably a smidge too much chilli pepper!

recipe card for a manitoban classic. 

made for dipping chicken fingers but also great for fries and honestly lots of stuff it’s a great dippin sauce.

i actually make mine with half mayo half miracle whip and it’s surprisingly better.

I was really in the mood for some comfort food and my house, that's usually a pasta dish. Tonight I made shrimp linguine. The sauce was made with butter, shrimp font, spinach, parsley, a heap of parmesan reggiano grated on a microplane, crushed garlic, lemon zest, and pinot grigio.

Wishful thinking it was Friday, I prepared a Poke bowl with salmon drizzled in a Yuzu mango dressing. Paired with steamed broccoli, tangy pickled red cabbage, edamame, lightly sautéed radishes, pickled red grapes, and sushi rice. All I need is a Lei to wear while eating it. 

GohanClub! FoodSky

In Japan, you can almost always find this type of egg bread at local bakeries. It's bread filled with mashed egg, and it's almost always tasty. I assumed it'd be sold in other countries too, but my friend in Canada told me they don't have anything like it. 😱

We always made homemade pizza on Christmas Eve, and then a very suburban Ohio dinner at my grandparents’ Christmas night. This combines the two. 
Ham, black and green olives, gherkin pickles, potato chips on the crust and there's Pepsi in the pizza sauce. (Tomato paste, Pepsi, mustard, and garlic)

My foodie friend made this sublime dinner last night: roasted garlic, caramelized leeks with morels, bok choy, spinach, watercress asparagus, bone broth with butter, cream, fresh rosemary, sage, thyme and cannellini beans. And maple bourbon sriracha shrimp. And she deglazed the pan w/ white wine. 🤯

Necessity (in this case--meds need low-sugar food) is the mother of invention. We had leftover fry bread, pica de gallo, eggs, and cheese. I put them on in that order, nuked them for a minute to get the eggs started and they went into the air fryer for a few minutes. So good and so quick.

A salad of pinto beans, pan-fried corn, red onion, cherry tomatoes, & the pickled jalapenos that were brined with some fancy pickles I bought at the farmer's market. Tossed it all with brown rice, lettuce, & some sweet/savory dressing and ate it with jalapeno cheddar bread & (yes, more) corn 😋🍜🍽️

bouillon is always my go-to but you can always punch up "box" stock by just boiling it ahead of time with vegetable trimmings day of with stuff you already have. If you have the space, saving your trimmings in a freezer bag is the way to go, otherwise, just buy a nice thing of Better Than Bouillon

Old fave unhealthy snack for the game: dump a whole lot of peeled garlic gloves in some olive oil and wine and whatever spices you like.  Bake low for 3 hours. Spread like butter on toasted French bread. Drizzle with pesto and some Parmesan.  Cut into bites.

Quite proud of the potato dish I made. Sort of a gesture at au gratin, the sauce is - rather than a sauce mornay - stewed onion, garlic, and fennel blended with Dijon mustard and 1/2&1/2. I then mandolined the potato, and covered with pecorino toscano, cheddar, and panko. @@4133

I have some words. A few will understand. A crispy fried egg in butter. The edges of the egg are golden brown. A red country sausage, split in half fried in butter so it's charred on both sides. Now served on the plate there's a heap of salt butter & pepper grits. And that's a breakfast of the gods.

How to cook with Laoganma crispy chilli oil.

1. Heat pan, and add Lauganma crispy chilli oil. spread around.
2. Add ingredients, and add Lauganma crispy chilli oil to taste.
3. Serve, garnish with Lauganma crispy chilli oil.

Made a modified version of Food52's recipe for Spaghetti Napolitan (Japanese Ketchup Spaghetti) — a dish popularized in Japanese cafes in the 50s.

Made from butter, sauteed red pepper, ketchup, sugar, reserved pasta water, and a splash of milk, it tastes like a grown-up version of Spaghetti-O's.

Rigatoni with braised chestnut mushrooms, roasted shiitake mushrooms, porcini mushrooms, and spinach, in a wild mushroom pesto, yoghurt, mushroom ketchup, and mascarpone sauce, topped with parsley, veggie bacon, baked marinated portobello mushrooms, and truffle oil.

I Like Mushrooms. 🍜🍽️

Last night's attempt at Ottolenghi's green chilli con carne. Not particularly green, and hotter than I wanted - I was hoping to taste more of the coriander and lime. Still, not even close to the deadliest spicy thing ever cooked in this house, and a perfect excuse to eat soft pittas as a side. 🍜 🍽️

Tonight's dinner 🍜: Xôi mặn (Vietnamese sticky rice). So good! I couldn't find pork floss or chả lụa (Vietnamese ham) when I was out shopping for the ingredients yesterday, so those are home made. This is one of the best things I've ever eaten.

I love mayonnaise. it's delicious. I like to mix it with ketchup or a hot sauce or both and dip french fries in it. or dip french fries in plain. I like it on sandwich and mixed in with can tuna or potato etc.

🍽️ 🍜 sirloin steak tips in a wine pan sauce i definitely didn’t ad-lib after realizing i lacked the correct wine served with a side of blanched & pan seared broccolini that i also didn’t cockup by nearly burning all my garlic and tossing still-too-wet broccolini in the pan to stop it. There are two kinds of hot sauce people — the first kind likes a watery consistency, like Tabasco.

The second likes a thicker sauce, with a consistency somewhere between a syrup and a sap.

Objectively, people in the first category are wrong.

tenderised scotch fillet (salted & pounded then marinated in worcestershire sauce, soy, olive oil, balsamic, black and white pepper), grilled pineapple, caramelised onion, melted (df) cheese, homemade plum chutney, garden lettuce & tomato, sesame seed bun & aioli. yes i'm hot no you can't marry me

Fresh pasta dough made with rehydrated porcini mushrooms and filled with creamy roasted mushroom paste, finished in a brown butter sauce with garlic, splash of brandy, couple tbsp cream, some water from the porcini, fresh thyme, pecorino.

Crunched up corn chips on the bottom. A layer of refried beans, then Vidalia onion chopped up, cheese. Nuke in the microwave then top with salsa and sour cream if you have any. Full protein and tasty!!

I once had a sandwich in Manhattan that was bubbling hot cheese scraped onto a baguette. Everyone's seen those, but this one was different. As part of the show, they brought out a line of women in sequined leotards who did high leg kicks throughout the meal.

It was a day to make more of my home made Bolognese sauce. 2 lbs ground beef, 1 lb minced sweet Italian sausage, 30 oz crushed Roma tomatoes, 30 oz tomato sauce, 2 cups minced onions, 1 cup minced garlic, oregano, thyme, basil, sage, rosemary , black pepper to taste.

Denny's Human Torch Skillet (2015-2015): Part of the "Slamt4stic Menu" for the film "Fantastic 4," this item featured a huge breakfast sausage, seasoned red potatoes, sautéed mushrooms, bell peppers & onions, jalapeños, pico de gallo, a five pepper sauce, Pepper Jack queso & 2 eggs

We’re back with another installment of ugly food! This is beef au poivre and cheesy potato gratin with pear and pecan salad. As you can see, the beef is a little over cooked and the gratin is a victim of my questionable knife skills. But everything tastes good!

Portobello mushrooms stuffed with wild mushroom-spinach-& leek risotto, tofu ricotta, & vegan mozzarella topped with panko breadcrumbs & scallions served with a balsamic drizzle and steamed green beans. #veganfood 🍽️

Had a go at recreating the burnt onion side you get in some Turkish restaurants last night and fucking hell I am a genius. Grill a wedged red onion until completely burnt on the edges, smother with pomegranate molasses & a splash of red wine vinegar, add loads of sumac & parsley and tuck in.

Cork-grown padron peppers from Gort na Nain via the My Goodness stall at the English Market, fried in a blistering hot pan with a little olive oil + Achill #smokedseasalt. A beer on the side (Eight Degrees Brewing's #DevilsLadderTripel FTW) to douse the flames.

Step 1 of my #vegan chili. The home made sofrito sauce. Roast onion and garlic, blended with tomatoes with fresh cumin and coriander spices. Then red sliced chili peppers added.

Maharashtrian veggie delights at Croydon's new Shree Krishna Vada Pav - a comforting misal pav (sprouted bean curry with crunchy sev, coriander and buns); sabudana khichri (tapioca, potato, green chilli & peanuts); and the eponymous vada pav (deep-fried potato patty & chutneys in a bun).

I ignored my love handles and was silently extolling the virtues of the Italian sausage in the leftover casserole my mom packed for me and that I ate for lunch today. Because holy fuck, Italian sausage is delicious!

This coming Friday is an annual event I host for my son called Fryday. Very original I know. We do fried chicken, onion rings, fried pickles, French fries, deep fried Oreos and maybe some fried cheese curds. What are your go to fried items?

I just had a great 2-weeks of being a kid again (all my dinners made, no decisions, rides to and from the train so I could visit friends) but now I am home and making the most basic spaghetti-and-jarred sauce for myself and that is also very nice.

I spent a lot of my life thinking I hated soup - seemed like it was just a bland, watery grave for overcooked vegetables and pasta. But making soup from scratch at home completely changed my opinion! This intensely flavorful pasta e fagioli with fresh crusty rolls is one of my favorite meals. ❤️

Latest addition to my imaginary breakfast sandwich, coffee shop and newsstand dream business is the McDiamond. English muffin toasted with a light schmear of maple syrup on top, two baked eggs, cheddar cheese, and a fried piece of beef salami.

Its getting slightly colder right now and the world is burning so I made a massive pan of imam bayildi, and its tomato-ey, garlicky, olive-oil richness is just what I needed to feel a little bit of comfort

I’ve got a pork butt slow cooking in the oven and it’s making the house smell so good. ☺️It’s braised with broth, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, honey, red pepper flake, & sesame oil. I’m going to shred it and make lettuce wraps with it with a sesame carrot slaw, cilantro & green onions. 🍽️🍜

I'm a pretty good cook. The dish I am most proud of (not pictured) was the lasagna I made with fresh home-made pasta, fresh home made ricotta, and herbs and tomatoes from my garden.

The green papaya salad at Thai Nyyom made my lips burn. Chef Oat gave me a sample of the fried pork belly (along with my chicken and rice). It was so good — the tamarind-lemongrass sauce was fantastic. She is a master. The place was packed. Best Thai food in Toronto.

Back on my savoury porridge bullshit and can confirm that making the porridge with miso soup paste and stirring through some peanut rayu is probably the best idea I'll have in 2024. This one is topped with kimchi fried tofu and soft boiled egg and is the lunch of champions.

I'm sure you know the story. Even if I avoid overeating at this time of year, by now I am craving very plain foods to counter the treats I've been feasting on for days.

Today's recipe fills the bill perfectly. It's tabbouleh, a Middle-Eastern cracked wheat and parsley salad. 😋

Picked up a shoulder of lamb from Otway Prime and cooked it. It’s the simplest possible thing — an onion, eight garlic cloves, white wine, stock and a tiny splash of pasatta — probably less than a tablespoon. That’s it. Really not a lot of liquid. Five hours at about 140. So good!!

The dino nuggets and crinkle fries were microwaved to perfection. They was paired with a fine glass of apple juice and a chocolate torte (pudding cup). Afterwards everyone retired to the sitting room for cigarettes

Last year I was roasting squash (halved, cut-side down, as one does) and figured I might as well make use of the cavity, so I tucked a chunk of leek under each squash half and ended up with PERFECT roasted leeks.

I have been forgoing our annual Winter Solstice trip to Cossetta’s, since 2020 and Covid and recreating our favorites from them at home. I have the artichoke salad marinating and will do the portobello mushroom ravioli salad a bit later tonight. Pizza dough is in the fridge.

Meal planning for the week. Tonight, I’m making ricotta balls in a red sauce with a chopped romaine salad. The rest of the week: sesame tofu w/veggie fried rice and roasted vegetable and grains salad with feta. Didn’t intend to go vegetarian this week but just worked out that way. 🍜🍽️😊

The citrus/kiwi salad last night was a huge success. I used to make something like this years ago, so glad to come back to that one and improve it. Very light and fresh so breaks up a multi course dinner.

Making meatballs and sauce tonight even though the whole family is complaining about it. They need it! And there is leftover Roberto, too. I just got back from a surprisingly quiet Sunday night at the grocery (thanks, NFL!) and I'm just about ready to cook.

Main course is a rare rump roast with a sweet potato and carrot mash seasoned with sage and white pepper. A creamy whiskeysauce, steamed broccolini and brusselssprouts sautéed in bacon fat along with crispy fried diced bacon.

Bucatini all'Amatriciana tonight! Originally from the Sopranos cookbook (laugh if you will, every single recipe I've tried from it has been a BANGER) but tonight I'm using some modifications from Serious Eats.

Available at Japanese convenience stores, this boiled egg is a testament to Japan's exceptional hygiene standards and culinary skill. The yolk is expertly cooked to the brink of setting, infused subtly with salt during the boiling process in delicately salted water. Just yum, no more! 🥚

Stew mostly prepped for cooking for dinner tomorrow. Brisket joint defrosting to seal in the morning, then will add that whole, plus half a litre of stock and cook it all day in the slow cooker. Homemade dumplings will be added in the last 20 minutes. Marvelous.

Re-learning some basic cooking skills with low vision, today’s experiment was omelette.

The key is preparation, that’s true of any cooking, but even more so when simplicity is so important.

I couldn’t see how much cheese was on the cooking omelette, next time pre measure.

No burns = success.

FODMAP quick air fryer recipe favourite is boneless chicken thighs: preheat @ 400F, cook for 22-24mins depending on how stuffed your basket is.

These are tossed in an egg wash, then coated in brown rice flower and favourite herbs.

Always super moist and flavourful!

Here's my homemade cheese sauce recipe:

2 tablespoons butter
1 tablespoon flour
1 tablespoon shortening
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon onion powder
1 teaspoon black pepper
3 cups of whole milk
2 bags of shredded cheese

Cook on low-medium in a sauce pan, stirring with whisk consistently

kasha with cremini mushrooms (criminals?), thyme, shallots, garlic, mirin wine, fennel, pecorino romano, finished with a lemon, with homemade veggie stock (which has dried mushrooms in it). to be honest I find buckwheat a little dry and might go next time with barley, but it's its own thing

🌱 The pink bumblebee is here. You can see some pink pigmentation on this fruit but this is still mostly orange. As it ripens the color will change to more pink strains.  This was the best tasting tomato I grew last year.

🌱 Macintosh apple starts seem to sprout more quickly than whatever a “Johnny Appleseed” variety is. But the Johnny Appleseed variety is about to break the surface. 

The grow lamp is working its magic!

My primary reason for being on Madeira was hoping to encounter one of the rarest native European orchids, Madeiran endemic Goodyera macrophylla. Only a few dozen plants are known to flower annually, and in dangerously inaccessible places. Seeing this plant was a moment of sheer euphoria.

Despite the chilly cold front this week, new patches of toothleaf goldeneyes along the Salado Greenway went in for a fall bloom! These attractive asters grow in bunched colonies and can be up to 3-6 ft tall. I think I saw three or four patches in flower while out on my run yesterday. 🌼🌿

🌱 The bosque blue tomatoes are starting to ripe. The bottom of ripe tomatoes turn from greenish to a red hue. The top part remains the dark side of blue violet, about the color of eggplant. These are larger than the strawberry blk cherry tomatoes, but the taste is kind of unmemorable.

Watermelon seedling finding a new home in thick organic mulch, a mixture of dried grass with cow and donkey manure.

We are doing a trial to determine whether the manure is broken down enough to support seedling growth.

If it is, and we get fruits, they going be delicious! 🌱

Boring pepper update from my office garden: jalapeños are doing very well, cayennes have mostly sprouted and are generally looking healthy, habaneros have reluctantly pushed out two tiny seedlings and decided that is quite enough.

This little tomato right here is the sweetest I have ever had not because it is the first of many to come or even its sugar content but because it taught me a lesson That is in less than ideal conditions in my living room of all places I could grow food that nourishes my mind and body 🌱

It’s remarkably easy to grow the most expensive spice on Earth in your home garden. 
Saffron grows from bulbs / corms. They bloom in the Fall and produce 3 threads that you want to carefully hand harvest on flowering day. Dry in parchment paper and place in an airtight jar. 🌱

Two years ago, I planted passion flower seeds, but they didn't germinate — due to weather conditions. One seed found its way to the base of a small tree and sprouted. The vine began climbing the tree. This year the tree is covered with passion flowers and passion fruit. A delightful turn of events!

🌱 I'd like to pin this on my hair! 

This is the apricot lemonade cosmos. The flowers can change to become more pink than yellow at various times, or the other way around. Very muted pale colors that looks particularly lovely when grown next to lavender and dahlias.

My other half got this “Waxed Amaryllis” at Trader Joe’s. It grew as promised & is in full bloom. It's lovely, but I hate “disposable” plants. I’m going to try to liberate it from its plastic jacket, get it into a pot, & save it. #Hippeastrum ‘Christmas Gift’ #Amaryllidaceae 🌱

My toddler loves cherry tomatoes so much that she doesn't let them get fully ripe.  As soon as they're red enough, she demands her tomato tribute.

I have only eaten three cherry tomatoes that were fully ripe the whole season so far.

I think this is a sign to plant more next year. 🌱

Although they do produce seeds, trembling aspen reproduce almost entirely by cloning. This is a great example of new clones ￼shooting up into a once open meadow from the roots of the trees behind them. 🌿

This summer I grew alpine strawberries for the first time. Not only did they taste much more delicious than my regular strawberries, they were easier to grow from seed and produced constantly from spring to the last frost. I got a far bigger harvest from these than from my regular ones. 🌱🍓

Born in the tropical forests of Sri Lanka True (Ceylon) Cinnamon is well suited to grow in our zone 9b. This tree can grow up to 50 ft, or be pruned to live well in a container. It will not tolerate temperatures below 40 degrees, be sure to protect it from rare frosts. 🌱

I was in ATX over the weekend visiting some good friends! I went on a chilly morning run at the Lake Creek Trail and came across THESE. 

Maxamilian sunflowers are fall-time bloomers native from NM, USA to SK, CN + MO, OK & TX, USA, and have a wide naturalized distribution eastward. 🌻

🌿

🌱The Mexican sunflowers are just starting to bloom next to the lavender at the neighbor's farm. The flowers are small like daisies, and usually abundant. Not as tall as the perennial sunflowers I'm growing the last two years.

Happy New Year! Hoping for something completely different? How about Passiflora foetida the Stinking Passionflower? This one was blooming at the base of a heiau at La Perouse Bay, Jan. 2019. Certainly one of nature’s weirder floral arrangements!Wishing everyone a fun new year, maybe not this weird.🌿

🌱 The "bosque blue" tomato is shaping up but compared to the black strawberry cherry tomato I grew last year, there is no shade of blue here, just a dark, almost black color. Have not tasted it yet but will likely grow the blk starwberry again instead of the bb next year.

Native to the Mediterranean, Oregano loves to grow in sunny Florida! Give it a big spot in well-drained soil and it will flourish. After flowering the plant dies back some, but can still withstand a hearty trimming. Prune to a small bush as it can get leggy and try to spread. 🌱

📷: Tanria Elementals

🌱 I still suck at growing from seed but this sunflower is the one thing that's made it, can't wait to see what colour it is when it blooms

And a bonus one my roommate (not on here) grew that's very pretty

Last year I grew sunflowers. They didn’t grow very well because I planted them too closely hoping for more than I could sustain. This sunflower self-seeded from last year. Because I’m a poet, this is a metaphor about intention.

My tomato plants have turned into tomato bramble because this is my first time going tomatoes outside and I didn't know they were going to turn into a large bush. I'll remember for next year. I will get those fancy tomato cages and adorn the tomatoes in the finest silk 🌱

Was so concerned my tomatoes wouldn’t ripen in time, I didn’t immediately realize I had no idea what colour Sungold tomatoes should be when ripe besides yellowish orange. When a fruit fell from the plant, I figured that was the sign they were ready. 

I did it! I grew tomatoes! 😊🌱

🌱 Of all the volunteer tomatoes I have this year, this is the most surprising. This is a Black Strawberry Cherry tomato growing by my garden gate which has mostly crushed rocks. I did not plant this variety this year but here it is!

Scrambled eggs! Scrambled eggs! 🍳 This cute native wildflower is often called “scrambled eggs” or “yellow Corydalis.” Across their native range you can find them blooming in and around rocky areas, creek beds, and hillsides from late winter through early fall as temperatures and conditions allow! 🌿

🌱 The wild columbine is blooming all over the garden. A native which is not planted so much as exuberantly spreads itself by seeds. Not hugely invasive and easy to pull up if it lands somewhere you really don't want it but a lovely early spring bloom to enjoy while waiting for other plants to bloom.

In awe of how beautiful my dahlia blooms are. These are my first foray into anything more complex than throwing some allysum seeds down, so the fact they’ve been so colorful is really encouraging. Can’t wait to really dig in to container gardening next year. 🌱

New blossoms are starting to open up! I wasn't sure what to expect when I brought this baby home last month, it was in the discount section, looking a bit yellowed and haggard. It has perked up so much! greened right up, and is now busy filling my home with the scent meyer lemon blossoms 🌱

Papaver somniferum (Opium Poppy), love her crinkled petals when she has just emerged.  These grow well in my garden and I just scatter the seed in situ.  They are some of my favourite blooms.  🌱

The family of Hawthorn (Crataegus spp) plants is large, but the members all have one thing in common - they’re pure magic! They grow almost anywhere and prolifically produce super healthy flowers and fruits. It’s prime berry season so let's get to know one of the oldest medicinal plants.

I absolutely did not have time to do this today, but temperatures are supposed to dip this week, so I harvested all of my remaining balcony cherry tomatoes. I lost about a third of the fruit because they ruptured in the rain, but I'm happy with this haul.

Sunflowers may be the symbol of Ukraine, but they're native to North America, where they were first domesticated in the Late Woodland period.

Seeds found in Mexico are all pre-contact, suggesting Spanish priests suppressed them to block their importance in Sun-worship cults.

Decided to plant potatoes on a whim in an overgrown area of the property surrounded by hedges. Had no idea if it got enough light or had good enough soil. Of all 3 areas I planted them, it’s flourishing best here. Sometimes rolling the dice pays off. 🌱

yellow jessamine | Gelsemium sempervirens 🌿

one of the great plants of the deep south, but it only gets into the southeastern corner of Virginia, and is not common.

After a month of searching the Appomattox valley, I found enough seed for my friend to try to grow at her local native plant nursery.

Once the primary 🌱 agricultural focus of the region, #Meghri #Armenia has has shifted away from Soviet-enforced pomegranate orchards to the more profitable (and perishable) persimmon.

Deprioritized, pomegranates still get packed - but no new orchards get planted. Legacy trees straggle along.

it’s the first wildflower of spring, although it doesn’t look like it! 🌿

Symplocarpus foetidus | skunk cabbage is a wetland plant that can generate their own heat and are the first eastern plant to bloom each year. The stinky little dome flowers are pollinated by flies. 

Araceae, like Monstera

A lovely old French iris from 1927, 'Floridor'. It has a wonderful, heavy grape fragrance, but its best feature is the unique shade of blue, often described as slate blue in the old catalogs. It really stands out in the garden and is such a good grower and bloomer.

Recently, I have been looking into the Late Pleistocene climate and vegetation of Argentina for a work project, and was delighted to find out that pink peppercorn was - and still is - part of the native flora in the area. Maybe Macrauchenia developed a taste for the spicy tree.

Two years ago when we moved here, this hydrangea was one single branch with a couple leaves. I thought it would not make it. The following summer, it had a tiny bloom. This year it has exploded in size and blossomed beautifully. We will have to move it this fall as it is crowding the gate 🌱

Ilex dedidua | possumhaw 🌿

This little tree was less than a meter tall and growing out of a crack in a mossy granite boulder and was still laden with fruit for migrating birds.

This is one of our rarer hollies in eastern Virginia, and has the odd distinction of being deciduous

Parochetus communis 🌱

This unique, spreading clover produces single gentian-blue flowers, as opposed to the florets containing many tiny flowers found in true clovers (Trifolium).

Beautiful and nitrogen-fixing!

What a difference a day makes. First one is my sunflower looking a little bucktoothed with its two petals yesterday. Second was the flower this morning. 

I think the colors here are going to be gorgeous when fully bloomed.

i’ve grown nasturtium in this barrel both the last two summers but this summer i have been rotating the barrel a quarter turn every day when i’m watering (takes a bit of muscle! 💪🏻) and the plant is much fuller and nicer 🌱✨

Potatoes were cultivated by indigenous people across the Americas, but likely 1st domesticated in the Andes region of Peru 7-10,000 years ago from wild species Solanum brevicaule.

Cultivated potatoes are all tetraploid, while wild potatoes can be diploid, tetraploid, or hexaploid.

We have a ridiculous volume of California bluebell seeds and the temptation to seed-bomb with them is pretty high. Who wouldn’t love them? Good foliage, they die back completely to make room for summer blooms, and that BLUE—it’s unmatched 🌱

Today's Gardening Advice: Siberian Iris Paprikash is one of the stranger Siberian Iris I have. The flower color is decidedly different. Goes well with other colors in the garden. Like other Iris relatives it prefers full sun and tolerates dry when established. Not picky about soil. 🌱

🌱 Eastern black swallowtail/ Papilio polyxenes asterius (Stoll) finally alights on a red dahlia/ Dahlia parvibracteata (Saar & P.D. Sørensen) so I can take her picture. 🦋
Black swallowtails, pipevine swallowtails, and monarchs, as well as many bees favor this nonnative to my area single dahlia.

Daffodil bulbs planted in compacted soil vs bulbs in normal soil. 🌱

The compacted one is under a sycamore tree with good branches for climbing next to a freshman dorm, and the other one is 20 feet away without good climbing branches.

After winter sowing over 500 plugs last year, this year I only managed 3 milk jugs: Purple milkweed, blue Prairie sage, and native passionfruit vine. More evidence of how much my health has declined in the last year. I hope the new shed helps me renew my strength. 🌱

I love this dahlia bloom (Cen has been working on developing different varieties and it's fascinating).

This one in particular is such an intense reddish-orange color. Like a saturated Cinderella pumpkin color.

Had a literal field day with strawberry growers near Charlotte NC!

NC grows the 3rd largest strawberry crop in the US after CA & FL. But unlike those states, most NC strawberries come from small farms.

Gangly wild August garden. 🌱 Butternut squash ripening on the vines just everywhere. Tomatoes fully escaped all the supports I can think of. Watermelon hanging on. A couple of happy pumpkins. Not bad for year one.

The alpine strawberries we uncovered when rescuing the patio from under the rubble and detritus have been absolute stars. Fruit every few days for almost two months now, feels like they are saying “thank you!” (Or possibly: “oh heck we need to spread seed everywhere before that happens again”) 🌱

Today's Gardening Advice: Check seed packet date ranges prior to starting seedlings.  Some vegetable seeds germinate quickly and grow just as fast.  Too often I hear from people, "Oh, I started my tomatoes in January and now they're all leggy." Yes, wait, read directions, don't start too soon. 🌱

Today's Gardening Advice: Need a flowering tree for a small space? This is Cercis canadensis 'Covey', also sold as 'Lavender Twist.' Flowers form all up and down the stems and the weeping form reaches about 10'. Prefers full sun to partial shade.  They don't tend to do well with dry soil/hot sun. 🌱

More signs of spring:
1) daffodils really putting it out now
2) hellebores (lenten rose)
3) flowering quince
4) apricot blossom (peach blossoms should be blooming soon; later pear & tart cherry, then last are the apple blossoms)

Last year’s dwarf irises—among my very favourite plants—were a treasure. This year we learn whether they’ll perennialize in the spots I picked for them, but as insurance, we planted another fifty. There can never be too many! They take up no space; their post-bloom foliage is narrow and subtle 🌱

Many micro-farmers will tell you that their most profitable crops are salad greens. Think about how much you pay for a bag of greens that lasts you a couple weeks and makes a few salads.

Growing your own greens is fun, easy, and saves $$$!

I counted 77 blooms on the early daffodils I planted two years ago. They are multiplying very slowly. At the water’s edge the sea kale is sprouting. The plants have small leaves of two or three inches; they are a deep purple. 

Modern Nature —

My mom gave me a cutting from her Christmas cactus years ago. We had it for a couple years, and it barely did anything. In 2021, I moved it into a window with better light, and it started growing like crazy. This is the second year it's flowered, and it makes me so happy to see :)

Corn plant propagation going well now that I've added an aquarium pump to the vases. Propagation station looking healthy. The schefflera, on the other hand, is getting cramped. Should have pruned it months ago. 🌱

Planted a Little Lemon clematis in a tall pit along a sunny hog wire fence. 🌱 I’ve tried a few annual vibes here without good results, so I’m at peace with the fact that this is a non-vining clematis (learned after planting).

The Rainfall tree begins its full blooming session in January.
This one is a bit early, the flowers are beloved by honey bees.
Also called 'quickstick', livestock farmers plant the sticks as poles and have them as living fences, an additional source of protein for their animals.

I succumbed to Black Friday and got myself more Greenstalk self-watering planters. Highly recommended if you are short on space or have mobility issues. I grew a ton of tomatoes, beans and herbs in mine and want to expand.

Not sponsored or anything, I just love them

🌱  Behind the tomatoes there is a hardy strawberry in full sun. Its two siblings are in the raised bed but I kept this one here and it grows a ripe strawberry every few days.
Very hardy plant, tasty sweet fruit.

I am selecting plants for the next years, this strawberry is here to stay.

While they love to grow in limestone, the Carnation doesn’t like the overly hot Summers of Florida. If you want to enjoy these blooms through Spring, best to plant in Fall. Small bushy plants that do not like wet conditions, be sure to monitor moisture levels during wet season!

These are the berries of trees growing along the edge of the marsh adjacent to my property.

Google identifies these as Red Chokeberries, but I’m not 100% convinced. They’re 20ft tall slender trees and make only a handful of berries each year. 🌱📸📷

One of our patches of Blue-eyed grass is blooming nicely even though it has been cool and mostly cloudy lately. It looks like grass (except for the blooms) but is in the Iris family. 
2nd photo is a bloom closeup. 🌿 #NativePlants Southern California

Belle de Jour floribunda rose looking fabulous after overnight rain. A lovely apricot fragrance, she is full of blooms & will continue flowering until the first frost. Rose of the Year, 2021, bred by Maison Delbard.

We had a lemon tree on the balcony that outgrew its pot. We gave it to the caretaker, who planted it in the garden of our block of flats. This year it’s groaning under the weight of dozens of lemons so I’ve just been down to relieve it of part of its burden. The smell in our kitchen is unbelievable.

It’s either pouring rain or 100 degree heat index here in Nash. The basil went from beautiful to black overnight, squash bugs finally got the cucumbers, jalapeños are tiny, tomatillos huge but scarce. Tomatoes are surprisingly fine but must ripen indoors?

My nasturtiums, which had done nothing for the most part since I first planted them in May, are suddenly going wild now that it's nearly November. Climate change, man. Everything is weird now. (Bonus Harvey in this pic because it's his morning meditations spot.) 🌱

My garden adventures were kind of a disaster thanks to the horrific unending rainfall of the summer, but I did manage to grow potatoes!  They’re beautiful!  I’m so proud.  I also grew some really pathetic daikon radishes

The rapid spreading ability of Goldenrod makes it a perfect plant for abundance and luck. Associations with Venus make a powerful ingredient for love magic, and stems can be used as dowsing rods. As the name Solidago implies, protective Sun energy is strong with this yellow flower!

since i might lose all my blooms to tomorrow’s power washing disaster, i cut some for the first time. they’re too small & too few to do much with, but i grew them from seed and they’re very cheerful 🌱

The first harvested Sweet Sunset. It's starting to ripen and starting to get a little wrinkly around the damaged part so I decided the time was right.

Overall, a moderately sweet pepper (at least at this ripeness) with a very mild and very fruity flavour.

🌱With notes of 🍋 Lemon & 🌹 Rose, fragrant Hippeastrum 'Apple Blossom' (Ludwig & Co., 1954) is a favorite Amaryllis worldwide, can increase into BIG clumps. Left in cool 3-Season Room colors become deeper. Try outdoors Zone 7 at a South wall, Zone 8+ in the open. Amaryllidaceae

You can’t tell me this isn’t one of the most beautiful plants on the planet, each one is different even between same batch seeds with tight genetics

🌱 I planted some apricotta and xsenia cosmos this summer. I think this one is the xsenia but it looks darker than the photo in the seed packet. I love it anyway, so will be collecting seeds for planting next year.

One thing I'm especially proud of with my garden is that I grow it almost entirely with reclaimed water. 💦

I live in CA where water conservation is a big deal, and we save every bit of water we can.

I have ~20 plants, and I've only watered with the hose twice this year.

The precious Paperwhite Narcissus grows well in Florida, as long as you give it a cooler location to live in. Being one of the few varieties that doesn’t need chill to flower, they will bloom in clusters of showy, fragrant petals. Bulbs can be planted in either soil or a rock bed.

One of the easiest and most fuss-free varieties of cauliflower we have grown! Medium sized heads with kid-friendly mild flavor. Both heat and cold tolerant, though afternoon shade is recommended for hot southern climates. 🌱💚

With higher temperatures and less rainfall, we are forced to change the way we plant food.

This image shows a row of black eyed peas growing between 10 year old date palm trees. The area was not tilled.

I kinda thing this is how it was done before we got smart. 🌱

One of the 'easy Sunday' plans is to plant some banana and plantain suckers on the farm.
I will plant this one as well, silk banana or some call it golden banana.
It is not the best type of sucker to plant. This is a water sucker, the better ones are sword suckers but it will still grow. 🌱

This bulb was an impulse buy around Christmas. At the time I was too depressed to even read the instructions, so I plunked it on this wine coaster I had left out and forgot all about it. Turns out that IS how you grow an Amaryllis. I think? Seems like bad luck to read the growing instructions now.

🌱 A few of the happy blooms of the Mexican Zinnias for you. About a foot tall in a dry area with infrequent watering. Can more than double it's height when watered more often. Size of flowers remain small at about 2inches in diameter. A delight!

put two rose bushes in this year from plug plants and expected little other than hoping they’d establish well for next year, and they keep surprising me! one has bloomed twice, and even the shyer climbing rose has decided to give me a little spray of new buds in september 🌱

Question for the gardening folk: last year, my cosmos grew tall before they flowered—the ones on the sunnier patch got as tall as me and flowered pretty late into fall. This year, they have been staying much smaller, but blooming earlier. What might be the reason for this difference? 🌱

The new soil works! My tomatoes went from shrivelled to lush, sparse to abundant, fleeing this mortal coil to smothering my neighbourhood’s native flora. 

More than I can store. Family & friends won’t accept them. Cupboards are full. They’re in my bed. I dream tomatoes.

The vine is in charge.

Now that I like for some reason.

I found two pink katydid nymphs in the same spot in 2021, and haven't seen another since. That hasn't stopped me from superstitiously checking the same spot on the regular. The pink is a genetic mutation, and they're fine, other than not looking like leaves. 🌿

This 2nd year hydrangea has more than doubled in size. The flowers are all fairly low on the bush (which is where the sun hits), and there's another foot of greenery at the top, out of the picture frame, which is its way of telling me that it wants to be transplanted forward this fall. 🌱

I like growing peppers. And I feel a bit more accomplished this year because about half of the plants are raised from seeds from last year. Here are some cherry peppers and some poblanos that came from seeds from a grocery store pepper from last year sometime. 🌱

What a weird year we are having for gardens in Victoria. My place gets a lot of wind and only afternoon sun. But the strawberries are finally coming in — I have never had this many blossoms in my 3 years here.

And my “saucy Mary” green Roma tomatoes are my surprise performers. I need to stake it! 🌱

🌱 I planted some Two Tasty hybrid tomatoes from Burpee Seeds. In my area (NJ), they are performing well - fast growing & abundant with a decent cherry tomato flavor. The fruit size is about halfway between cherry and plum.

As you can see in the photo I took, they have an heirloom look to them.

I'm so glad I grew some melons in the greenhouse this year. 🌱 I've gotten multiple ripe melons from the greenhouse, which was such a striking comparison this morning, comparing the most developed melon out in the garden against this morning's breakfast.

🌱 I must report that the apples are now wearing a nice blush on! 

This is one of four columnar trees I planted last year as bare roots; all are fruiting this year with about 7-12 fruits each tree. Looking to harvest in 5-6 weeks.🤞

The case for planting Allium: they are stunning and hardy. They are onion-family and a natural pest deterrent. They naturalize and multiply! They bring lovely height to a garden. Bees and butterflies adore them! 🌱

We generally grow quite a few varieties of chilli peppers, but not so much this year. This time we limited to a few species including these freshly picked Thai Chiang Rai chilli peppers. They produce a medium heat and taste quite good with Asian dishes.

This cool rainy summer must have been ideal for raspberries because they started the first week of July and haven't stopped except for a week or so in August. TBH the freezer is full and I'm getting tired of them. I may leave the rest for the yellow jackets. 🌱

Romanesco cauliflower.

Wikipedia: "The inflorescence (the bud) is self-similar in character, with the branched meristems making up a logarithmic spiral, giving a form approximating a natural fractal; each bud is composed of a series of smaller buds, all arranged in yet another logarithmic spiral."

So I finally tried what the cool kids are doing and didn’t bury my seeds, instead I sifted my potting soil to make it soft, then put the seeds on top, and covered in  perlite.

Well the results blew me away 😳 these seedlings have only been growing for 8 days!

Recovering from learning:
1) Pineapples grow from shrubs (not trees)
2) The plant is a perennial and can produce multiple fruits, each taking about a year to grow.
3) Wild pineapples are pollinated mostly by hummingbirds.
4) Of course, there's messed up colonial history of pineapple monoculture.

🌱 I had planted 3 evening sunflowers but in a verrrry shaded spot just for fun. I wasn't expecting much. 2 of them got decapitated at one point. 1 survived. It didn't look like much yesterday. I thought maybe it was stunted from so much shade...

First wildflower of…winter??? Texas baby-blue-eyes are native annuals distributed from Texas, Oklahoma, Louisiana, to Alabama. They generally have an early bloom season, starting from around mid/late February where winter temps are moderate, through the end of spring, and dying back by summer. 🌿

Thyme is exceptionally easy to grow. It needs well draining soil and full sun, and loves to be trimmed. Waiting until just before flowering ensures the leaves have the most oil available. Our rainy season can damage the plant, but moving it indoors or away from the elements can help. 🌱

Coreopsis is a vibrant flower known for its yellow petals. It thrives in diverse soil conditions and withstands cold winters. It adds beauty to gardens, attracts pollinators, and creates colorful floral arrangements.

Ignore the fact my hand is all sweaty, I was finishing up a run lol…

Happy to report that it’s Texas persimmon (Diospyros texana) season! Persimmon fruits ripen around early August and are a fav among wildlife. TX persimmons have a small range from central and west TX through northeastern Mexico. 🧪

We’re here to share our passion for roses, gorgeous plants and gardens. Introducing ‘Meteor’ Rose of the Year 2024. 
The golden yellow-orange petals flush vermillion-red in sunlight. Glossy, lush & healthy foliage.
Bred by Kordes. 
Bare root & container plants widely available this autumn.

The strawberry plant was just an abandoned pot from the previous homeowner. We decided to take good care of it this year, and it has really done well. The fruit is a bit tart, but very flavorful. (the variety is Gasana) 🌱 #AmGardening

My 15-year-old jade plant recently went through a rough patch where she looked horrible— shriveled, drooping, discolored. Did you know that your plants’ soil can become hydrophobic? Well, a good repotting took care of that, and now my girl is back to her old self. Much to my relief.

Have you tasted Custard apple or Sitaphal.This fruit is power packed with calcium and several vitamins 🫶🌿🌱

I love its unique taste. It’s can be very easily grown from seeds and the small bushy tree yields fruit in less than a year or two🫶🙌🪴

Saturday Morning Garden Report 

A volunteer Korean melon vine is producing a melon! Paul Robeson tomato is getting larger, still very green. The volunteer cherry tomato might not be one of the tasteless hybrids - looks lt. orange, not red! And the Korean red pepper plant has lots of green babies.

There are so many things in my #BalconyGarden that I want to show Turtle, or ask her about. The sunflowers are on their way, the tomatoes are coming, the strawberries are doing so well. How do I separate strawberry plants? Miss you my friend. 🌱🇳🇿 🐢

🌱 The perennial sunflowers are starting to bloom. It's nice to see them come back for the 2nd yr. Had to deadhead aggressively last year as I didn't want it taking over the yard.I'm still hoping to find some Mexican sunflowers. Not as tall, comes back every yearly & can be planted as a nice hedge.

Onion seedlings on a window sill with a bonus blurry view of the back garden. I like growing onions from seed because you can try different varieties and grow them in clumps, these are new ones for us, “Long Red Florence”, or “Rossa Lunga di Firenze”, if you prefer.

🌺 little sweet betsy 🌿

from early april

different species & much more widespread than twisted trillium, which mostly stick to western middle tn & northern al. these are all over the south & follow the mountains up past new york

In today’s edition of Looking Through Garden Photos Thinking About Spring, I’m fascinated by the flower coverage on these two phloxes (each at its max flower power). Left phlox gets more sun, but not that much more. Curious to see if this repeats this year, as I prefer the right’s colour 🌱

🌱 First time harvesting more than a couple fruits from the columnar apple to trees. Should I pick and store then eat or should I pick and eat? I don't know if storing them would make a difference in taste, does it?

one of my cherry tomatoes died completely after the power washing fiasco, but all the survivors are showing signs of recovery. we lost a lot of foliage but the black krim is looking particularly robust! really looking forward to tasting this one 🌱

Second round of raspberries has been pretty mediocre, but the strawberries don’t seem to care that it’s getting cold and wet! Here’s hoping the peppers get a move on here soon though because that’s just the first two ripe on a bush with about sixty 🌱🌶️

Amaryllis doing a bloom! Looks very red there but it’s that amaryllis coral shade that’s the exact midpoint between red, orange and pink, and looks different colours in different lights. Love the patterning on the petals 🌱

Last few days above freezing. Pruned the calmondin back by almost half 🍊🌱(was growing weirdly anyway), shook all blossom bits down, gradually getting her ready to live indoors again.

Smells dreamy, I made enfleurage with calmondin blossoms in spring. Blooms every 4-5 months.

Something I did not anticipate saying: the asparagus crop is threatening to overwhelm us in volume. Him Here did put a lot of effort into getting it  established and planted something like 11 different varieties.

This is (finally!) the first bloom on one of the new dahlias I planted this year (Bliss from Swan Island).  As you can see it looks a bit beat up which is not uncommon for the first blooms IME. There are more buds so we will hopefully get some more! 🌱

Two more teeny tiny shishito peppers and the first sungold cherry tomato! The sungolds may be my absolute fave thing to grow because they're so tasty. I'd hoped more for the shishitos but the plants are out there flowering like crazy and living their best life so I can't be mad. 🌱

soo happy whenever this gets a bloom. I love the colors. its from a rose I have migrated from My late grandmother's garden. the plant itself is over 30 y.o. (she had it forever too). I have abused it (drought, heat, poor soil) but it persisits. 🌱🌹

this rose usually gets absolutely devastated by aphids but this year is doing well.  

i always give it a good prune but i cut it right back this autumn. is that what helped? more birds hanging around it too?

According to most guidelines, this snapdragon and this salvia, both of which I planted last year and both of which came back this year despite being “annuals”, are bittersweet. Like they’re very pretty but I don’t think we’re in zone 7 anymore where I live 🌱

Tomatoes are finally just going gangbusters in my Aerogarden. I should have been pruning more aggressively, but this is my first try growing a batch of them, and I'm pretty jazzed about my results, regardless. 🌱

Hello, friends! One of the highlights of the garden at the moment is a huge stand of these swamp sunflowers. They always bloom so late that we think they aren't going to bloom at all but then they go wild! Nice late pollinator and food source for wildlife. 🌱

Home from my almost all day scan. Now lying down. So sore. New sunflower is out, another is on it’s way and the initial huge ones are looking great. It was a three strawberry day in the #BalconyGarden 🌱🇳🇿

Fleabane daisies are really quite weedy plants, but I am continuing to like them very much. Also enjoying the aromatic asters, now that they've made it thru an entire summer of me wondering repeatedly if they were something I planted on purpose.

🌱 Some more dahlias have joined the flower party. I was in danger of losing the pink one but it made through and now here for the second year. The salmon and red colored ones were planted this spring.

Considered by some to be the most important plant for pollination in North America, the Goldenrod (Solidago spp) is a gorgeous keystone species. Providing food for hundreds of insects and animals, this fall blooming flower can grow prolifically, even becoming weedy in some areas. 🌱

It’s 34F outside, raining tiny beads of sleet, & my previously barren camellia is in full bloom.I have learned three things about this plant this season: it does not like fall pruning(LOL), it likes plenty of acid fertilizer, and & it thrives in cooler weather .🌱Flower Report  #Gardening #Nature

there's a lightly shaded area of my dad's lawn where the vines died off and left a barren patch, sandy soil has been washing away. i've staked some old chopsticks to stabilize the soil and loosely broadcast clover seeds along with some water.

I love the early spring blooms on this spirea. 🌱 Interestingly enough, I had 3 of these planted: 2 purchased from the nursery and this 1, propagated from a robust one I had at my old house. The other 2 struggled & are definitively dead now. Shows the value of cloning plants that thrive for you.

I threw a few collard starts in the ground 🌱 in early summer, and I wasn’t sure how they would do around/under my peppers. Well… now I have collards for dayyyyyys. This plant is almost up to my waist. I’m making saag paneer tomorrow to go with aloo gobi from our own potatoes.

We report: the mimosa trees are blooming bright yellow in the freezing rain. Every year when they bloom, we get surprised - middle of the winter, every other plant is putting its energy into braving the cold, yet the mimosa tree loudly bursts into song. We have the sniffles.

I'm impressed with these Aunt Molly's ground cherries. They were volunteer sprouts from old berries from the plant I grew in spring, AND a few weeks ago survived a light frost with only about half the foliage being lost! 🌱 

#AuntMollysCherries #groundCherries

🌱 Happy Friday! The flowering plum trees started blooming about a week ago; that’s a tad early this year. The budding branch in the foreground is from a Mt. Fuji cherry blossom tree. So the wait is on for those to grace us with its flowers.

Next up: the Texas yellowstar, native to Texas; Oklahoma; Arkansas; through to Coahuila. This pretty annual herb is commonly found in prairie-like habitats across north-central and southern east Texas, specifically along the Edwards Plateau. It does best in well-draining sand and limestone. 🌿⭐️

Today's Gardening Advice: Harry Lauder's Walking Stick is a neat shrub for winter interest. All twisty branches and early catkins. Japanese Beetles and sucker growth can be issues. Like full sun and average garden soil. 🌱

Thought you folks might like to see skunk cabbage. Yes, these are actually flowers. They are one of the earliest spring blooms, so early that they generate heat to melt through snow, if there is any. Which makes a little oasis for winter pollinators

Bought a couple of 4" pots of Phlox stolonifera (which are impossible to find as seeds, which usually means they're practically impossible to grow from seed), one of the creeping phloxes, this spring, and they seem to like where I put them. TIL: Haas Avocado trees bloom March - May with each flower lasting only 2 days. The flower opens in the morning as a female, and then the following afternoon as a male. Seems wild and while they self pollinate the best harvests come from cross pollinating. 🌱

My single cucumber plant may take over the world. It’s now over 6’ tall and 4 feet wide after training it up the lattice. The huge bushy tomato plants pale beside it. First watermelon of the season. Not as red and sweet as we like. I think we may have gotten impatient and picked it too soon. Got a late start planting so we've been waiting all summer for these. Threw the rinds into the woods and some deer came by and looked very happy to discover them! 🌱

Today's Gardening Advice: If you're prepping for spring seed planting, be sure to wash any previously used pots.  Use a cap full of bleach in a gallon of water and allow pots to soak.  Rinse them well.  Bleach bath helps remove mold and potential insect problems.  🌱

For some reason this fuchsia hanging plant has decided to flourish even with my lack of consistent watering and hanging in a place with too much sun (so my neighbor’s daughter can see/enjoy the flowers)🌿

Cardinal Basil! A highly fragrant basil that grows to 24” tall. Bright green leaves contrast large, wine red flower clusters that fade to a dusty plum. Beneficial to pollinators, aroma deters common garden pests. 🌱💚

Today's Gardening Advice: If you're looking for a deep red daylily, Buckyballs is an excellent choice.  The flowers are extremely dark and the foliage tends to be evergreen in warmer climates. It's not especially picky about soil (like most daylilies) but does prefer full sun.  🌱

Pink evening primrose! Although this spreading perennial has a wide naturalized range, evening primroses are considered native to only Nebraska; Kansas; Missouri; Oklahoma; Texas; though northeastern Mexico where extensive colonies occupy grassy fields. They bloom from late winter through fall. 🌿

The first California native wildflower to bloom from seed in my newly-created meadow section in my yard, Nemophila menziesii or Baby Blue Eyes. It’s a part of the Japanese-inspired native garden I’ve been working on for the last year after removing the lawn. 🌱

Did you know we breed our own “black” sunflowers? Our Dark Chocolate sunflowers grow to 6ft with multiple branching heads, purple stems, and are uniformly darker than most chocolate varieties on the market.🌱💚🌻

Start with a small act of anti capitalism.

Next time you buy a tomato, save the seeds. The slimy stuff on the seed is a sprout inhibiter. Put the seeds onto a tissue or paper kitchen towel. Save it. Dry it. Stash it somewhere dark. 

Next spring, soak in water and place in dirt and grow tomatoes

Rosa’s very supportive of her younger sis, who happens to tend to a large flower garden at their house. Samantha may be younger, but she’s capable of standing up for herself.

my greatest plant discovery to date, a county record for Iris verna | dwarf iris 🌿

a truly tiny plant with full iris flowers that loves sandy places. I found this, the bigger of two populations in Wise County, VA, in an old forest fire site that brimmed with botanical wonders

🌱 I grew some foxgloves last year and I adore their lovely spikes. I did not realized how many babies each plant get. I have now potted about a dozen small ones and getting ready to pot the larger ones! I may end up with three dozens new plants!

Proud of this Baptisia australis (false blue indigo) plant for exceeding 1 foot in height. I started my Baptisia plants from seed last year, and... yeah, it's going to be a while before they get to full size. Which is fine. I love growing from seed, even though native perennials are slow. 🌱

🌱 The cosmos had sporadic flowers this past summer. The last few days, it has been flowering furiously. It’s as if it could feel the end coming any day now and it’s flowering like there’s no tomorrow.

Montauk Daisy buds. These are always the last flowers to bloom in the yard, even later than the Chrysanthemums. In another week or so, these will be brilliant white patches of color against the green of the leaves. 🌱

The specimen continues to thrive despite the unforgiving conditions that are far from where it would normally thrive due to support from others around it. 
Oh, and this pineapple is growing too I guess.

I finally got some marigold and nasturtium seed packs from the hardware store. 
These, I will sow on the farm, they will go a long way in helping with pest control.
We are aiming for farming as natural as possible. 🌱 
#savesoil
#RegenerativeAgriculture

Some lilacs are tolerant of heat and can flourish (zone-pusher) in the hot inland valleys of Southern California, with just that right location. “Now that the lilacs are in bloom, she has a bowl of lilacs in her room.” - T.S. Eliot 🌱#GardeningFeed
 
Syringa x hyacinthiflora  'Matthews Purple’

Multiplier onions are a win! Bought these after learning about them from SkillCult. Did not disappoint. Started from sets 2 months ago. Harvested sustainably for gronions as they grew, and each single set tripled or quadrupled.

Anyone else growing multipliers / potato onions?
🌱

Got the onion, leek, celery, and some pansy/viola seeds started today. All tucked into LED light tower. Hoping we get a warmer, earlier #Spring this year so I can move them out to the greenhouse. We had so many cold nights in March last year my living room was a disaster. #Gardening #Minneapolis 🌱

My Dorito plant is ready to harvest. They do particularly well in urban environments.  Seed dispersal is achieved through human activity only. I grew "nacho cheese," but come I'm many other varieties.🌱

Christmas bloating done.

Couldn't stand the thought of the newly purchased piceas sitting in their overpotted state(which I suspected would be the case when purchased)

So got stuck in and put them into shallower, wide pots and took away all the excess mix that had buried their trunks.

🌱🇳🇿🪴
🌳✂️🌲

I have carried out an experiment. I’m planting multiple seeds straight in the ground in short beds, covering them until the birds can’t harm them. I can do this every 2-3 weeks and it’s a lot less labour intensive! And I get use use the whole seed packet.🌱

I like to think the citrus will be happier this winter with an east-facing window, ceiling light tube & grow lights. 🌱🌞🍋

Last year, the lemons taught us that overwintering in the kitchen with its north-facing window & single grow light was not the thing.

🌱 In #syracuse #ny this forsythia bush is blooming in my neighbor’s yard. It should bloom in the spring, so this is wicked early.   Flowers blooming out of season is a bad omen. For example, spring flowers blooming in the winter means the temperatures are too warm & that has lots of ramifications.

Thought for sure the random pumpkin & butternut squash seeds I’d saved from last year’s dinners wouldn’t germinate. 10 of the 11 grew! Moved 9 plants to spots around the property & kept 1 pumpkin in the container. No idea if they’ll produce fruit (or what exact type). But yay! 🌱

So excited! We just ordered a dozen new bare root fruit trees for our home orchard from Bay Laurel. They will arrive in March and we already have the irrigation in place. Also ordered a few more roses and an experimental cold tolerant Kiwi vine.

Holy moly. The Allowissadula holosericea flowered literally overnight in the natural area where I live. I went out this evening to do a quick trash pick-up before nightfall and these guys gifted me with a BEAUTIFUL bloom! 🌿 

My other favorite color is yellow. Purple + yellow = my color scheme! 😌

I mixed up a cup of herb seeds and scattered them in the front yard bed. This is my last attempt at a garden, just stirring together random seeds and scattering with a bit of hope. May the odds be ever in their favor

This snazzy little flower is a rain lily, Zephyranthes atamasca is native to a chunk of the Southeast but threatened in some of its range. I planted them lining the walkway in the back. They look like a clump of chives most of the year, but after a good rain, they flower (hence the name.)

So one of our wee fuschia that didn’t do all that well over winter has decided to flower, the colours are a lot brighter than last year. 

Plus the old pear tree is flowering prolifically, should be a good crop 🌱

Bought some blousy marigolds and spiky salvias for the garden. Trying to keep the local hover flies/bumblebees on-side (partially because I just love them and want them to hang out, but also I kind of sort of want them to pollinate my beans/squash plants).

Sowed my first Spring seeds at the allotment 😀🌱

This time each year, I put all my leftover salad stuff leaves in a packet, and sprinkle some every couple of weeks. Gives me signals on how the soil is warming, when and if they germinate. And I usually get some early salads, too. 🌱👩‍🌾

The Texas mountain laurels are officially in full bloom! Not a true mountain laurel, it’s a small shrub-like evergreen in the pea family endemic to central and west Texas through northern Mexico. Their blooming season (March through April) fills the air with a STRONG smell resembling grape soda! 🌿

Willows and Hellebores to get me through to spring

my late blooming varieties took a hit from the ice and snow we had, turned most of their blooms to mush and damaged early leaves 

early bloomers are more cold hardy though and coming in strong

🌱 The marigolds are my favorite companion plants or mass planted as stand alone plants. Been trying to plant the white ones with minimal success. I plant them every year in my veges garden and as a border in other parts of the yard.

What better way of cheering up a grey Monday than with a new rose! Gorgeous floribunda ‘Me and You’ ♥️ Plentiful deep-red blooms & healthy dark-green glossy foliage. 
Exclusive to C and K Jones Rose Nursery, Cheshire. 🌱

Harvested my Crapaudine beets today. It was an experiment...I tend not to have a ton of luck with beets because we have heavy soil. I had half and half luck with these-- maybe six or seven good sized beets and a bunch of small ones. I have a ton of greens, though, so that's exciting!

Oh I forgot one of my most esoteric accomplishments of the day: I found out at least two of my pawpaw seeds, after over a year of coaxing along, are sprouting. One from each batch, too, so I can get different genetics

This variety is a mutation of the species with no pigments in the flower other than yellow. I wish I also have the natural form, which has spots on the dorsal petal and more colors.

🌱 Paphiopedilum insigne var. sanderianum

#orchids #plants #gardening

Tromboncino Summer Squash! It's a zucchini-like summer squash - with a *twist* - demonstrated here by our youngest. It can grow up to three feet long, but you can harvest it at any stage before it is full size. 🌱💚

Promised giant white peony pics.🌱
These enormous white peonies came with the house. Along with some very nice magenta coloured specimens. This year Mrs D made peony jelly. It was amazing. We turned our 4 peonies into a veritable plethora of peonies in front of the pawtio for next season.

Blueberries had VERY productive season last year so let's hope for the same this year! Even tho one bush is spindly & one bush is fat 'n full, they're both amazing producers.  

#gardening #garden #joy 🌱

Made my boyfriend a revitalized growing spot for lavender! Dug up and repositioned the bricks and dug up the roots of the porcelain berry that used to be there and made sure to rake over to get any extra root or such off the surface so no weeds!

My hibiscus bloomed! My lemon tree came back from the brink of death. My morning glory has completely overtaken the trellis and is attaching itself to my lilac bush. And my cosmos are as tall as my house.

You forgot this: 🌱

We only buy cherry tomatoes because commercial slicing tomatoes are so disappointing. We also only eat big tomatoes from July-October when they're available out of our own garden. Sasha's Altai is our current favorite because it's adapted to a short, cool growing season.

Mums in the morning light: the 1st blossom of tiny "Herbstbrokat" is finally opening. And "Mary Stoker", one of the earliest Chrysanthemums who has been blooming in bright yellow for some reason, decided to finally produce the proper colour - and in a double blossom, too!

Mungbean will fall into dreamland after a long day of gardening - longer cos she forgot about DS.
Paid labour removing wandering jew, asparagus fern and ivy *for hours & hours* and the pleasure of transplanting seedling from old to new garden. Did the tricky surgery with the carrot seedlings too😊

A relative of the Nightdick is the Morningwood, displayed here.

While the Morningwood most frequently blossoms at first light, it is known to reveal itself following nearly any period of darkness, such as a thunderstorm or and an afternoon nap.

桃の花は割と短い期間しか綺麗でないので、見る時期が難しいものです。長野県の昼神温泉付近の花桃の里は、標高差があって、長期間どこか咲いていることが多いです。鯉のぼりがあるように、これは5月の写真です #flowers

Peach blossoms have a short blooming period and are difficult to see, but the flowers here can be seen for a long time (due to the difference in altitude). This is a photo taken in May

🌱The purple hyacinth are putting on a show today. The pink ones will be blooming nicely by tomorrow. We appreciate their lovely fragrance and good cheer. They keep the house from being dreary now that the Yuletide decorations are gone.

I nursed this bush back from death over the summer. The whole thing was brown sticks. I didn't even know it could flower. 

I'm bad at growing things and I have trouble maintaining the yard with my disability. And somehow, it blossomed in the fall anyway ❤️

First of the satsuma fruits 🌱🍊 are beginning to ripen. This, along with the key lime, will move into the greenhouse for the winter; it’s too cold here in 7a to overwinter outside. As long as we don’t get another brutal temperature drop like last year these will be very happy over the colder months.

Every year our biggest brugmansia waits until the first frost to pop off, so we are forced to keep this in our bedroom for a few weeks. Imagine someone punched a hole in a can of lemon pledge, but in a good way.

Found some photos to show from seed raising to planting and now
🌱🇳🇿
This patch is a bit weedy and construction happening behind, also slugs/snails love the rengarenga but once established it bounces back from the munchings
